title: Customer Service Specialist
description: **Customer Service Specialist – Call Centre Experience Required** **Location:** No. 2 Dublin Landings, 2 North Wall Quay, Dublin 1, D01 V4A3 **Working pattern:** On\-site, full\-time **Shifts:** During the first 3–6 months, working hours will be 11:00am–8:00pm. Following this initial period, shifts will transition to 2:00pm–11:00pm.​​ **Start date:** TBC **Training:** Full structured training programme provided FOODHUB IS GROWING — AND WE’RE BUILDING A CUSTOMER SERVICE CENTRE OF EXCELLENCE Following a highly successful recruitment campaign, we are expanding our Dublin customer service hub and are now looking for **experienced call centre professionals** to join our team. We are building a **high\-performance, phone\-first customer service operation** in Dublin and are looking for people who are reliable, resilient, and comfortable being measured against clear performance expectations. **The role** As a **Customer Service Specialist**, you will act as the **first point of contact for Foodhub clients via phone**. You will be expected to take ownership of issues end\-to\-end, make clear decisions, and escalate only when necessary. **This role requires:** * Confidence handling continuous inbound calls * Strong problem\-solving and decision\-making skills * High attention to detail * Comfort working to clear quality, productivity, and attendance standards **Key responsibilities** * Handle inbound customer calls with a strong focus on **First Call Resolution** * Take full ownership of customer issues from start to finish * Accurately log and update customer records and actions taken * Identify whether issues are Customer Service or Technical and act accordingly * Support clients with: * Orders and delivery queries * Account access and verification * Datman registration and basic troubleshooting * Carry out proactive outbound calls (check\-ins, promotions, follow\-ups) * Use phone, SMS, and email effectively to resolve issues * Work consistently to defined KPIs covering quality, accuracy, and productivity What we’re looking for**Essential (non\-negotiable)** * **Previous call centre or phone\-based customer service experience** * Proven experience handling high call volumes * Comfortable dealing with difficult or emotional customers by phone * Strong spoken and written English * High attention to detail and ability to follow processes accurately * Reliable attendance and punctuality * Comfortable receiving feedback and coaching to improve performance **Desirable** * Experience in payments, EPOS, or technical support environments * Experience supporting customers in time\-sensitive or high\-pressure situations * Hospitality or restaurant industry exposure (as a plus, not a requirement) **Our CORE values** We expect every Customer Service Specialist to demonstrate our CORE values daily: * **Care** – Treat customers with respect, even under pressure * **Ownership** – See issues through to resolution, not hand\-offs * **Resolve** – Focus on solutions, not excuses * **Educate** – Help clients understand the tools they use **What we offer** * €30,000 – €35,000 salary (DOE) * 33 days annual leave (including public holidays) * Option to buy up to 5 additional annual leave days * Paid day off on your birthday (when requested) * Discounted Bupa Select Complete Health Insurance * €35 monthly gym contribution * Full structured training programme * Clear performance expectations and progression pathways * A professional, high\-accountability call centre environment Final note This role is best suited to people who: * Have **prior call centre experience** * Are comfortable being measured and coached * Take pride in handling challenging customer conversations * Want to build a career in a structured, performance\-driven environment If that sounds like you, we’d like to hear from you. title: Data Analyst, Financial Enablement
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies \- from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups \- use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** The Internal System Org is at the core of Stripe to empower mission\-critical financial domains including Finance \& Strategy (F\&S), Accounting, Tax, Treasury, Product Experience, GTM. We are the Financial Enablement team, acting as the centralized data analytics team for Internal System Org. Enable and empower F\&S, GTM, Accounting, Product teams to achieve business goals, through dataset development, data analytics support, dashboards, AI, and operation efficiency enhancements. What you’ll do **Responsibilities** * Perform data analysis, identify trends, conduct research, build visualizations, all to deliver objective, critical insights that fulfill team stakeholders’ business needs * Work closely with cross\-functional teams, including Finance \& Strategy, Accounting, Product, Tax, User billing, Seller System, and Sales Ops teams, to align on financial goals and drive revenue growth * Be the data strategy partner for our business stakeholders * Reconcile complex dataset across complex finance systems * Coordinate cross\-functional projects to identify and drive operational efficiencies that support our stakeholders ability to run more efficiently and effectively * Work closely with Engineering and Product teams to help define product solutions * Leverage AI tools to improve team work efficiency * Conduct internal knowledge sharing sessions to strengthen team Who you are You’re passionate about solving business problems and making a direct impact on business. You thrive in environments with many moving parts and quickly pick up new domain knowledge and technologies (e.g. AI). We’re seeking someone with excellent communication and advanced SQL/analytics abilities. Strong background in Accounting/Finance or prior AI experience is highly preferred, though these can also be developed on the job. **Minimum Requirements** * Bachelor's degree in STEM major (i.e. Computer Science, Math, Stat, Engineering, etc.) and 4\+ years of prior working experience in data analysis fields * Strong analytical ability, proficiency with SQL (minimum of 3 years advanced SQL) and strong understanding of data relationships * Experience working with extremely large datasets * Outstanding communication skills * Expertise working cross\-functionally to drive business and operational goals * Excellent project management skills * Domain knowledge in Finance/Accounting/GTM **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience working in fast\-paced environments (e.g. high\-growth fintech/tech company, management consulting) * Experience working with PM, Engineers, GTM, Accounting Team or Finance Team * Experience working with financial datasets * Experience with visualization tools * Experience with AI tools (Claude Code, Kai, etc.) * Domain knowledge in FinTech Products * Qualification in Accounting/Finance or CPA/CFA certification In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €69,600 \- €104,400\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Digital Growth Specialist: Accra
description: *The ALX Ghana team is a high performing, excellence driven group that thrives on collaboration and scale. We embrace experimentation, take initiative, and go the extra mile to achieve results. Data driven and solution oriented, we hold ourselves and each other accountable to exceptionally high standards, fueling our consistent year on year growth.* *Do you thrive in fast paced environments where experimentation, optimization, and measurable results are the norm? Are you a creative problem solver who enjoys building and scaling digital systems that drive real growth? Do you have a sharp analytical mindset that helps you uncover insights from data, optimize campaigns, and improve conversion across the digital funnel? Are you someone who enjoys combining creativity with technology to design innovative marketing strategies powered by AI and modern digital tools? Are you comfortable rolling up your sleeves, testing ideas quickly, and iterating until you find what works?* *If so, then this might just be the role for you.* **About ALX Africa** ALX Africa, a non\-profit organisation under the ALX Foundation, is dedicated to unlocking the potential of Africa's digital future. Formerly part of Sand Tech Holdings, we've embarked on an independent journey to provide world\-class tech skills training and career acceleration programmes. Our mission is to bridge the digital divide, upskill and reskill talent, and create a generation of innovative leaders. **By 2030, we aim to empower 2 million Africans to secure sustainable tech careers.** With hubs in 8 cities across Africa and counting, we provide safe access to quality learning and a dedicated network of expert instructors. Our innovative programmes equip learners with the practical skills and knowledge needed to succeed in today's rapidly evolving tech industry. Through a combination of rigorous coursework, industry partnerships, and hands\-on projects, we prepare our students for in\-demand roles in software engineering, data science, and cybersecurity. **We Achieve This By** * Providing young professionals with access to the most in\-demand tech skills that will power the future. * Empowering the next generation of technology innovators, entrepreneurs, and business leaders through challenging, real\-world coursework. * Building a lifelong, impactful community of tech professionals that support them at all stages of their career journey. **Our Impact Thus Far** * 347k\+ graduates since 2020 * 257k youth in work * 31k youth starting own ventures * 60k youth in jobs created by entrepreneurs Visit our website **www.alxafrica.com** to learn more about our digital revolution. **Role Summary** We are seeking a highly driven **Digital Growth Specialist** to lead and execute our digital growth strategy across multiple platforms. This role is responsible for designing, implementing, and optimizing data driven digital marketing initiatives that accelerate growth, increase engagement, and improve conversion across our digital channels. The ideal candidate is both analytical and creative, with proven experience in performance marketing tools, SEO, paid advertising, and website optimization. They should demonstrate a clear creative instinct for campaigns, messaging, and content, and be well\-versed in using AI and legacy technology for growth marketing. This is a **hands\-on execution role** for someone who enjoys building, testing, and scaling digital systems that drive measurable results. **Specific Responsibilities** * Digital Growth Strategy and Execution: Develop and execute data driven strategies to increase traffic, engagement, and conversions across digital channels, while continuously testing and optimizing campaigns to improve performance and return on investment. * Performance Marketing: Launch, and manage performance marketing campaigns across platforms such as Google Ads, LinkedIn, TikTok, Meta, WhatsApp, and SMS, optimizing targeting, messaging, and budgets while running A/B tests to improve acquisition and conversion. * AI Driven Marketing: Identify and implement AI tools that improve marketing efficiency, personalization, and campaign performance, using AI for audience insights, automation, content generation, and campaign optimization. * SEO and Organic Growth: Develop and execute SEO strategies to improve search visibility and organic traffic through keyword research, content optimization, and ongoing performance monitoring. * Website and Digital Experience Management: Manage and optimize the website to ensure a seamless user experience, keeping content current while improving usability, user journeys, and conversion pathways. * Creative Campaign Development: Develop creative ideas for digital campaigns and growth experiments, developing compelling messaging and collaborating with internal teams to deliver engaging marketing initiatives. * Analytics and Reporting: Track and analyze key digital performance metrics, building dashboards and reports that provide insights into campaign performance and inform data driven optimization **Skill Requirements\-Must Have** * 5\+ years of experience in digital marketing, performance marketing, growth marketing, or a related field * Hands\-on experience managing paid campaigns across platforms such as Google Meta, TikTok Ads, etc * Familiarity with AI tools and their application in marketing, automation, and campaign optimization * Solid understanding of SEO principles and organic growth strategies * Experience using marketing analytics and reporting tools * Experience managing or optimizing websites and digital user journeys * Experience with marketing dashboards and data visualization tools * Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and optimize campaigns * Excellent problem\-solving skills and a strong test and learn mindset * Ability to work independently and execute initiatives from concept to completion * A creative mindset with the ability to generate innovative marketing ideas and campaigns **Skill Requirements \- Nice To Have** * Background in content marketing or digital storytelling * Basic graphic design skills and familiarity with design tools such as Canva * Experience working with marketing automation tools **Person Specification/Attributes** * Courage: Willingness to speak up, challenge the status quo, and embrace new challenges. * Humility: Openness to learning, seeking help when needed, and a focus on serving others. * Adventure: A passion for setting ambitious goals, tackling difficult tasks, and finding joy in the journey. * Initiative: Proactive problem\-solving, a sense of ownership, and a willingness to go above and beyond. * Resilience: The ability to bounce back from setbacks, persevere through challenges, and emerge stronger. **Employment Type** This is a full\-time role.

Our teams continually strive to find better ways – always searching, never settling – to achieve extraordinary results. Our values around hiring, training, and community engagement reflect a company culture that is inclusive and forward leaning, always pushing the limits of what is possible. **We as an organization celebrate the values of inclusion and equality, and advocate for the full participation of all people in an environment free of discrimination. To support these values, we invite all qualified applicants to be considered for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, citizenship, age, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.**

title: Electrical Engineer
description: **The Opportunity** **THE OPPORTUNITY** At Stantec Ireland, you’ll join a growing Energy team at the forefront of a transforming transmission and distribution sector. With rising demand and ambitious decarbonisation targets across Ireland , this is an opportunity to contribute to critical infrastructure shaping the energy transition. As an Electrical Engineer specialising in HV cable design, you’ll work on a diverse range of projects including HVDC interconnectors, offshore and onshore wind, substations, and grid connections—supporting initiatives aligned with EirGrid ’s network development. You’ll collaborate within a multidisciplinary team across all project stages, from FEED to delivery, contributing technical expertise while developing your career in a supportive, innovation\-driven environment. **About The Role** ou will take on a key role within our growing Energy team, supporting the design and delivery of high\-voltage cable systems across a range of major infrastructure and renewable energy projects throughout Ireland . Working as part of a collaborative, multidisciplinary environment, you will contribute to projects spanning HVDC interconnectors, offshore and onshore wind farms, substations, and grid connection schemes. Your responsibilities will include supporting and leading design activities across all project phases—from feasibility and FEED through to detailed design and delivery—ensuring high\-quality, efficient, and innovative engineering solutions. You will work closely with internal teams and external stakeholders, including key clients such as EirGrid , helping to address complex technical challenges and deliver infrastructure that underpins Ireland’s transition to a more sustainable energy future. This role offers the opportunity to apply your technical expertise, mentor junior engineers, and play an active part in shaping impactful projects, while continuing to develop your career within a supportive and forward\-looking organisation. **YOUR IMPACT** In this role, you will help deliver safe, reliable and sustainable water infrastructure for communities across Ireland. Through your expertise in process design, you will develop effective treatment solutions that improve water quality and support resilient systems for the future. Working closely with multidisciplinary teams and clients, you will contribute to innovative, practical solutions across the full project lifecycle. Your work will help ensure designs are efficient, compliant and deliver long\-term value while supporting the collaborative culture that defines our team at Stantec. **About You** You are a proactive and technically capable Electrical Engineer with a strong understanding of the electricity transmission and distribution sector. You thrive in collaborative, multidisciplinary teams and are confident coaching and supporting colleagues across the wider team. You will ideally hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Electrical Engineering (or a related field) from an accredited institution, with membership of Institution of Civil Engineers (EI) is preferred. You bring experience in civil substation engineering and a broad awareness of HV civil engineering projects. You have hands\-on experience in the design of transmission and distribution systems across all stages (Concept, FEED, and Detail Design), applying international standards for utility clients. You can coordinate effectively across disciplines, resolving design issues and ensuring practical, innovative solutions. **Your Technical Skills Include** * Cable installation design, including routing layouts, trench/trenchless sections, bonding and phasing, pulling, termination, and jointing arrangements. * Proficiency with CYMCAP software for cable sizing calculations to international standards. * Strong computer literacy in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook) and design tools such as AutoCAD or MicroStation. You are adaptable and capable of managing challenging workloads under changing priorities, delivering high\-quality results in a dynamic environment. **WHY JOIN US** * We put people first: We're proud of our friendly, inclusive and collaborative environment where everyone feels valued and driven to succeed. * We are driven to achieve: Named in the top 50 companies to work by Glassdoor 2025\. Recognised by Corporate Knights as one of the world's top 10 most sustainable companies 2025\. \= * We do what is right: Industry leading training and development as well as paid for professional subscriptions. A professional progression plan, supporting you to become chartered. * We are better together: A supportive and collaborative team environment to assist with the growth of your career. * Growth: We're on an exciting growth journey across the Ireland \- we want you to be part of it! * Great Benefits: Competitive salary, enhanced pension plan, annual leave with the option to buy/sell additional days, private medical insurance, group income protection \& life assurance included as standard! **About Stantec** Whether you’re starting out on your engineering career or are looking for a fresh challenge, Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, is a great career move. Based in Dublin, Galway, Castlebar and Cork, we are actively recruiting for team players with experience in any of the following areas – water and wastewater treatment process and network design, integrated network management, structural design, site development, road and bridge engineering, project management and administration. You’ll be working for a dynamic company with an excellent reputation in the industry. Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, offers a great working environment amongst friendly professionals with an emphasis on teamwork. It’s a company where your ideas and opinions will be valued and your efforts recognised and rewarded. We have been awarded the silver Investors in Diversity accreditation by the Irish Centre for Diversity for our commitment to quality, diversity and inclusion (EDI) in the workplace.

title: Electrical Project Engineer
description: **Electrical Project Engineer** Location(s): Dublin, Cork, Galway, or Kilkenny **Join us in shaping the future of electrical building services and infrastructure delivery.** This is an opportunity for an ambitious Electrical Project Engineer to contribute to high\-quality, technically robust projects across healthcare, public sector, renewable energy, and wider building services programmes, with the support of experienced senior engineers and a collaborative multidisciplinary team. **Purpose \& Scope of the Role** The Electrical Project Engineer will support the delivery of electrical building services projects from concept through to handover, working under the supervision and mentoring of a Senior/Principal Engineer. The role will contribute to feasibility studies, option appraisals, scheme and detailed design, using calculation spreadsheets, specialist design software, AutoCAD, and Revit. You will also visit sites during construction to monitor progress and quality of installation works, while working closely with internal colleagues and wider project teams to help deliver successful outcomes for clients. **Your Impact in This Position** In this role, you will help turn design intent into practical, buildable solutions that meet technical, regulatory, and client requirements. Your work will support the delivery of safe, efficient, and well\-coordinated electrical systems, while contributing to project quality, programme certainty, and strong client satisfaction. You will also play a part in the development of major infrastructure and building projects, helping to shape sustainable long\-term delivery. **Core Capabilities** **Technical Expertise** Strong technical ability across electrical engineering services and design, including MV/LV distribution, lighting, fire alarm, ICT, and life safety systems. Knowledge of Irish electrical design standards and building regulations is essential. **Software \& Digital Design Tools** Experience using AutoCAD, Revit, MS Office, and specialist electrical design software such as Amtech and Dialux to support high\-quality design delivery and documentation. **Communications \& Collaboration** Excellent communication, organisation, and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and work effectively within multidisciplinary project teams. **Project Delivery Support** Ability to contribute to project delivery across all stages, from feasibility and concept design through to construction support and handover, while maintaining quality and attention to detail. **Site \& Construction Awareness** Practical understanding of construction\-stage activities, with the confidence to attend sites, monitor progress, and support the quality of installation works. **Qualifications \& Experience** **Expected** * Minimum Level 8 qualification in Electrical Engineering, or equivalent such as Energy or Building Services. * Minimum 4 years’ experience in electrical building services design. **Beneficial** * Progress toward chartership is strongly preferred. * Equivalent work experience will be considered where appropriate. **Working Environment** This role is based in Dublin, Cork, Galway, or Kilkenny. It operates on a hybrid basis, typically requiring 2–3 days per week in the office, with remote working available where appropriate to project and team needs. Occasional travel to client sites or other offices may be required. Flexible working arrangements are supported where possible, including bank holidays and Fridays, with a focus on sustainable delivery and professional wellbeing. **Why join Tetra Tech?** Tetra Tech is a leading provider of consulting and engineering services working across the full project lifecycle worldwide. We have a wide range of expertise across our teams, providing a global support network with a personalised approach to client relationships to better understand where we can add value. We provide a collaborative environment that supports individual performance, innovation, and creativity. W e support public and private sector clients on local, national, and international projects to deliver sustainable and resilient solutions. Our operations in the UK, Ireland, and the Netherlands include more than 6,000 employees who are Leading with Science® to solve our clients’ most complex problems. In alignment with the Equality Act 2010, we will make reasonable adjustments to support candidates and employees requiring additional arrangements. This could include adaptations to work schedules, training approaches, or the physical workspace. Please inform us if you need any accommodations during the recruitment process or in your day\-to\-day role. **Recruitment Agencies** We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on this list and any submitted will be taken as a gift. Please refer to the careers page on our website if you require any further information.

title: Engineers – Engineering, Asset Management & Offshore
description: We are at the heart of Ireland’s energy transition \- connecting, operating and transforming the electricity grid to support a low carbon, renewable future. We are building talented teams that tackle real world challenges every day and help us to achieve our goal to prepare the grid to carry up to 80% of Ireland’s electricity from renewable sources. We are proud to be a certified Great Place to Work, recognised for our culture built on trust, collaboration and inclusion. At EirGrid you will find supportive teams, opportunities to grow and meaningful work. We also offer secure employment with a range of flexible and family friendly working policies to support your life and wellbeing. If you want to know more about what EirGrid has to offer you, visit Life at EirGrid. If you are ready to make an impact and grow your career in a purpose\-led organisation, your journey starts here. **The Opportunity** We now seeking Engineers to join our Engineering \& Asset Management (E\&AM). We have variety of new roles from junior engineers to senior lead engineers for the following areas: Client Engineering, Asset Management and Offshore. The successful candidates will perform engineering assignments to ensure that business priorities are met. The client engineering function is responsible for undertaking project design reviews/site inspections on both contestable and non\-contestable transmission projects to ensure that projects are developed in accordance with EirGrid functional specification standards. Delivery of these projects is required to meet key departmental and company objectives. **The Team** The Engineering \& Asset Management is responsible for undertaking four key roles; asset management, technical authority, onshore engineering and offshore engineering. The team is responsible for setting the technical engineering policies and standards to be used for transmission infrastructure, monitoring compliance during design and construction and for ensuring asset integrity and ensuring uninterrupted power system functionality. The Engineering and Asset Management and Offshore Delivery are the areas within EirGrid which facilitates that role through the development of technical standards and functional specifications for all plant to be connected to the transmission system and the development of new and innovative technologies and methodologies. In addition, these areas are responsible for ensuring that EirGrid’s requirements are incorporated in project design and construction whether the works are carried out by ESB Networks or by third parties. **About you** Successful candidates will have:* Electrical Engineering qualification to degree level or equivalent qualification, typically with 4\+ years relevant post\-qualification experience. * Good technical knowledge in high voltage transmission design with strong knowledge of transmission technology including new and emerging technologies. * Transmission Substation design expertise with experience assessing substation designs for conformity to standards at 110/220/400 kV. * Excellent communication skills with an emphasis on high quality report writing and ability. * Experience preparing and issuing transmission design drawings, such as single line diagrams, substation layout drawings, protection schematics and interlocking drawings, etc * Experience completing onsite inspections and monitoring of construction progress for compliance. * Full drivers licence. **Closing date for applications** 5th June 2026 *Please note we receive a high volume of applications, and as every application is carefully reviewed our response time may vary. If you agree to be considered for other roles this may result in a delayed response as we establish the best fit for your experience. We appreciate your patience.* We value potential as much as experience. If you believe you can succeed in this role, we encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every requirement. We're dedicated to diversity and inclusion at EirGrid. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and the importance of a supportive culture for all our people to achieve their potential. Our diversity and inclusion networks have been designed to create an inclusive culture that enables our people to feel a sense of belonging at work. As an equal opportunities’ employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process.

title: Executive Assistant to Chief Executive Officer
description: **Job Title:** Executive Assistant to the Chief Executive Officer **Location:** Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid: 3 days/week in\-office minimum) **Employment Type:** Full\-Time, Permanent **Visa Status:** Applicants must hold an existing, unrestricted right to work in Ireland. Visa sponsorship is not available. **About BrackenRidge** We are BrackenRidge. We make intelligent software infrastructure for the energy transition. Founded in Ireland, BrackenRidge is building AI\-native operations software for companies that operate and participate in modern energy systems — helping them navigate increasingly complex grids, markets, and operational environments. Our mission is to build world\-leading intelligent software infrastructure for the energy transition — software that does more than process data. It understands energy systems and markets, anticipates operational risk, and augments human expertise across every layer of energy operations. **The Role** We are looking for a foundational Executive Assistant who thrives on keeping the trains running on time in a high\-performance startup environment. Working directly alongside the CEO, your primary mission is administrative optimisation: keeping the paper moving, shielding the CEO's time, and organising incoming operational traffic. You will also support the CEO as he liaises with third\-party service providers (legal, HR, accounting, and serviced office spaces) while acting as the frontline coordinator for our rapid recruitment and interview pipelines. **Core Responsibilities** * **Executive Support \& Gatekeeping** : Manage the CEO’s administrative workflows, filter requests, coordinate correspondence, and optimise executive time tracking. * **Vendor \& Partner Support** : Act as the primary point of contact for external professional services, including accounting firms, corporate legal counsels, HR consultants, and office providers. * **Hiring Pipeline Support** : Run the initial coordination, scheduling, and tracking of candidate interviews as BrackenRidge builds out its core team. * **Office Operations** : Maintain the operational integrity of our Dublin workspace, ensuring seamless workflows, vendor management, and internal record\-keeping. **What We’re Looking For** * **Startup Pedigree** : Proven experience working as an Executive Assistant, Office Manager, or Operations Coordinator to a CEO/MD within a fast\-moving, high\-growth startup environment. * **Multitasking Mastery** : A natural ability to wear multiple operational hats seamlessly without losing attention to granular details. * **High Accountability** : Discretion, strong corporate execution skills, and a proactive drive to keep complex administrative systems organised. * **Process Excellence** : Outstanding written communication and a structured approach to document management, scheduling, and third\-party vendor coordination. * **Positive, Collaborative Mindset:** A personable, proactive team player who is willing to turn their hand to anything and who brings energy and optimism to the workplace. Someone who builds strong relationships with colleagues and stakeholders, and approaches challenges with a can\-do attitude. **Our Culture \& What We Offer** At BrackenRidge, we are building a high\-potential, fast\-moving environment where you will collaborate directly with a dedicated team of market and technical experts. Working with us offers a broad array of hands\-on experiences across the cutting edge of the energy transition. Our culture is built on extreme collaboration, where everyone is expected to participate fully, share ideas openly, and actively lift each other up. We balance our rigorous, high\-performance standards with a supportive spirit of continuous learning, mutual respect, and shared fun. In exchange for your expertise and commitment, we provide a highly competitive remuneration structure designed to reward your impact: * **Financial Rewards** : A highly competitive base salary matched with performance\-driven bonus opportunities. * **Time Off \& Agility** : generous paid annual leave, coupled with a commitment to supporting flexible working arrangements as the pace and rhythm of our scaling startup activities settle. * **Professional Growth** : A dedicated annual training and development budget to support your continuous education and technical mastery. * **Team Alignment** : Engaging team events and structured semi\-annual company offsites to connect, align on metrics, and celebrate our shared milestones.

title: Executive Assistant – Commercial (Pensions Group)
description: ### **Description** Our Pensions Group is one of the leading specialist teams in the Irish pensions sector. The Group has considerable experience providing advice on all aspects of pensions law to a wide range of corporate clients, public sector and former public sector clients, pension scheme trustees and scheme members. The Group provides the full range of pension advice including advice on scheme design, scheme set up, scheme administration, scheme mergers and winding ups. In addition, the Group advises on the pension aspects of mergers and acquisitions including the conduct of due diligence, the negotiation of related warranties and the merging or de\-merging of pension schemes. We advise on a range of issues dealing with scheme administration and governance issues on a day\-to\-day basis. We also advise employers and trustees generally on all regulatory and compliance aspects of pension scheme administration including the handling of complaints and Pensions Authority enquiries and investigations. We advise companies and trustees on the full range of issues in relation to all aspects of occupational pension provisions. **About the Role** At Arthur Cox, our Executive Assistants are the bedrock of our business. We are determined to support the career aspirations of our ambitious and talented employees. To do this, we develop specialist industry knowledge within our teams. This also allows us to offer comprehensive market\-leading services to our clients, continuing the success and growth of our business. You will have a solid and proven track record as an Executive Assistant. This is a permanent position. https://www.arthurcox.com/services/pensions\-and\-employee\-benefits/ https://www.arthurcox.com/services/commercial\-contracts/### **Key Responsibilities** * Providing comprehensive administrative and secretarial support to partners and associates in the Pensions practice group and wider Commercial team. * Comprehensive email and diary management * Manage AML requirements for client onboarding, ensuring all procedures are followed * Management of billing process to include time entry, invoicing, client account balances and follow up on outstanding payments. * Managing audit/accrual requests for clients * Maintain electronic files, ensuring all records are up to date and easily accessible. * Production, editing and formatting of legal documents and templates * Digital dictation * Co\-ordinating travel arrangements, meetings and itineraries * Processing Expenses * Maintaining and updating client database * Ad\-hoc duties as required ### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** * 4\+ years’ experience as a Legal Secretary PA/EA * Pensions experience beneficial but not essential * In\-depth experience with Client Billing/Payments systems * Excellent IT Skills in Microsoft Word, Excel and Power point * High degree of attention to detail * Typing skills of at least 70 words per minute * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills * Excellent organisation skills with the ability to prioritise workload * Team player, who can also work on their own initiative * Excellent oral and written communication skills ### **Benefits** * Competitive salary and bonus structure * Comprehensive health and wellness benefits * Professional development opportunities * Hybrid working arrangements ### **About Arthur Cox** Arthur Cox is one of Ireland’s leading law firms and provides a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks and financial institutions, established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. Our story has been defined by our progressive outlook. For over 100 years, the firm has led the way in the legal field in Ireland, delivering the best service and support for our clients. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. *Arthur Cox LLP is an equal opportunities employer. We value diversity and encourage applications from all sections of society. Please advise if you have any additional requirements, we will happily assist. Any information disclosed by you in this regard will be kept strictly confidential and shared within the firm only as necessary and with your consent.* *Reasonable accommodations provided for the purposes of your interview or which you may require during your employment will not be taken into account in hiring decisions.*

title: Fashion Brand & Sales Development Assistant
description: **Fashion Brand \& Sales Development Assistant** ​**Company**: Silk \& Sequins **Location**: Carlow Town, Co. Carlow (Remote\-First / Hybrid) **Job Type:** Part\-Time, Fixed\-Term (4\-Week Initial Trial ) **Hours**: 5 hours per week (Highly flexible scheduling) **Salary**: €14\.15 per hour (\+ 8% holiday pay) \+ 10% commission on all successful retail accounts secured. ​**About Silk \& Sequins** ​Silk \& Sequins is an established fashion house transitioning its signature heritage of bespoke, high\-end tailoring into an exclusive, ultra\-luxury **Ready to Wear** line. Driven by high\-octane glamour, structural drama, and unapologetic femininity, our collections focus on premium garment architecture and show\-stopping evening wear. ​As we scale, we are launching a targeted wholesale strategy to place our pieces into premium, independent luxury retail boutiques across Ireland and Europe. ***We are seeking a sharp, stylish, and self\-motivated assistant to act as the commercial engine behind this launch.*** ​**The Role \& Future Opportunity** ​This is a **micro\-part\-time, remote\-first role** (5 hours per week) designed for an ambitious fashion/marketing student, recent graduate, or freelance professional who wants to build an incredible portfolio. ​**We are explicitly looking for a long\-term partner to grow with the brand.** To ensure a perfect mutual fit, the role will begin with an initial 4\-week trial period. Upon successful completion, this position is intended to transition into a permanent, long\-term role as our retail network expands. ​You will work directly with the Brand Founder to manage B2B outreach and elevate our brand presence. This role is a perfect blend of digital research, creative luxury marketing, and real\-world commercial pitches. ​**Key Responsibilities:** ​**Wholesale Market Research:** Identify, vet, and map out high\-end independent boutiques across Ireland that cater to premium occasion wear and luxury evening wear clientele. ​**B2B Sales Outreach:** Build and manage structured Excel/Google Sheet lead trackers, drafting and executing sophisticated pitch emails to retail buyers. ​**In\-Person Boutique Pitching:** Visit target luxury boutiques—either alongside the Brand Founder or independently—to present the collection, display the garment architecture, and secure wholesale accounts. ​**Brand \& Content Alignment:** Collaborate on creative ideas for our social media channels to ensure our digital presence looks pristine, high\-end, and highly attractive to boutique owners. ​**Studio Alignment (Occasional)**: Avail of flexible, periodic visits to our Carlow studio to review new garment architecture, understand the craftsmanship, and capture authentic behind\-the\-scenes content. ​**Requirements**: ​A profound understanding of, and passion for, the luxury fashion industry (evening wear, premium tailoring, and high\-end design). ​Confident, professional communication skills with the ability to pitch to retail buyers face\-to\-face and via email. ​High organizational discipline—you must be comfortable managing spreadsheets and tracking leads accurately. ​Familiarity with digital content tools (e.g., Canva, CapCut, social media editing) is a strong advantage. ​Based in or within reasonable commuting distance of Co. Carlow for occasional studio alignment and regional boutique visits. ​**Benefits**:A direct 10% commission on the wholesale value of all successful accounts you secure. ​**Long\-Term Career Growth:** The opportunity to establish yourself early within a growing luxury brand with a view to long\-term employment. ​**Ultimate Flexibility:** Choose your own working hours weekly to easily fit around your college classes or other freelance projects. ​**Direct Mentorship:** Work one\-on\-one with the fashion designer and founder, gaining rare, face\-to\-face experience in luxury retail placement. ​**To Apply:** ​Please submit your CV along with a brief note detailing your passion for the luxury fashion industry and why you are drawn to the world of high\-glamour evening wear. ​Applications close within 7 days. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a brief 15\-minute introductory video call. Job Types: Part\-time, Fixed term Pay: €14\.15 per hour Benefits: * Employee discount * Flexitime * Work from home Work Location: Hybrid remote in Carlow, County Carlow

title: Finance Business Partner
description: **About Ethos** Ethos is a Greek word meaning "character". It is used to describe the guiding beliefs or ideals that characterise a community, nation, or ideology. Ethos are mechanical and electrical consultants to the data centre, commercial, high\-tech healthcare, and pharmaceutical sectors. Our mission is to engineer systems that complement the purpose of each building we design, improve the immediate and surrounding community, and meet the objectives of its operations, inhabitants, patrons, operators, and staff. We are an ambitious, innovative, and driven. We are passionate about delivering excellence for our clients and their projects, and we never settle for delivering work quickly at the cost of quality. Our work environment is positive, professional, and supportive. We take our work seriously, but we like to have a lot of fun too and we place huge importance on having a great company culture. We have been recognised as a Great Place to Work in 2019\-2020 and in 2020\-2021 and were awarded Best Company to Work For 2022\. **About the role:** Ethos prides itself on delivering efficient, effective solutions across a wide range of industries. We’re currently searching for a qualified accountant to join our ranks as a Finance Business Partner and continue our tradition of success. This person will play an important role across all areas of finance for the entity, as well as supporting the FP\&A and Operations teams and other Ad hoc tasks as necessary. **Role Specification** **Candidate Attributes** * The candidate must be based in Ireland to be consider for this position * 2\-3 years post qualified experience (PQE) essential * Ideally working in an industry role in construction or service provider * Experience working in the UK market desirable but not essential * Strong commercial acumen * Good organisational skills, able to prioritise effectively \& escalate as necessary * Proven track record of strong work ethic. Clear examples of “rolling up sleeves and getting stuck in” * Proven track record of problem solving with clear examples * Proactive \& effective communicator, able to communicate both internally within the business and externally to clients \& customers * Team player, ability to work both independently and collaboratively with other team members, in pursuit of finance objectives * Strong IT skills, including a proficiency in the use of excel. Experience working with financial systems / ERP a must * Continuous improvement mindset **Your Day\-to\-Day Activities:** * Own the role as finance business partner and point of contact for all finance related matters for Ethos’ UK based subsidiary. You will be required to present analysis, provide insight and make recommendations for future strategies * Assist commercial team with pricing, invoicing and cash collection * Own the accounts payable process implementing a purchase order process and keeping creditor balances within terms * Own cash flow management for the company reporting to the financial controller and managing director of the business on a weekly and monthly basis * Own the payroll process working with HR and external payroll provider to ensure accurate processing and payment of payroll on monthly basis * Take ownership of the company’s ERP system maintaining accurate master data and ensuring adequate training and resourcing is provided to the rest of the team * Play an active role in month end including: * Production of company management accounts within group deadlines * Ensure all areas are prepared to a high degree of accuracy * Work with the Financial Controller and other finance team members on timely delivery of reporting deliverables * Manage general ledger postings, reconciliations and journal entries * Work with local compliance partners of foreign subsidiaries around tax and statutory filings. * Take ownership of balance sheet reconciliations and ensure all completed accurately and on time * Support the annual audit process, liaising with external auditors and preparing necessary schedules. * Maintain and improve internal controls, ensuring adherence to company policies * Assist with budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis. * Provide financial analysis and reporting to support strategic and operational decisions. * Play an active role in new ERP system implementation * Occasional travel to the UK will be required * Assist financial controller and financial accountants with group reporting requirements * Assist Financial Controller and CFO with any ad hoc tasks. **Why Join us?** At Ethos, we’re dedicated to fostering an environment that encourages both **technical excellence and personal wellbeing**. **We offer:** * **Flexible Fridays** and a **9\-day fortnight working schedule** — enjoy every second Friday off to maintain work–life balance. * A **state\-of\-the\-art office in Charlemont, Dublin 2**, designed to promote collaboration, innovation, and wellbeing. * A culture that values **flexibility, learning, and teamwork**, with clear progression pathways and support toward **professional accreditation**. **Benefits:** * Flexible working times (in agreement with your Direct Manager) * Pension Scheme * Travel tax scheme or bike\-to\-work scheme * Regular training/mentoring / further college support * Subsidised Health Insurance Allowance * A Professional Subscription Fee paid by the company * Paid Statutory Leaves * Healthcare check\-ups and flu vaccinations * Gym / Fitness membership * Heavily subsidised social committee (5 a side, golf society, climbing club and regular social events) Here at Ethos we believe in equal opportunities and that each individual can bring something ‘WoW’ to our organisation. So if you don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, but think you have what it takes, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit. Our vision is to: “**Be the company people want to work with, and for**” Our values differentiate and focus us every day to be EPIC \- for our people and our customers.

title: Founding Engineer
description: Roughly a third of all the food the world produces is thrown away, and after households, the kitchens that feed us in hotels, offices, hospitals and universities are among the biggest wasters of all. A single commercial kitchen can bin tens of thousands of euro of food a year without ever seeing it on a balance sheet. It is one of the largest, most solvable problems in climate today, and almost nobody can measure it. Positive Carbon makes it measurable. Founded in Dublin in 2020, we build AI that sees what a kitchen throws away. Our sensor sits above any bin and uses computer vision, the same technology found in self\-driving cars, to automatically capture the weight, volume and type of every item discarded, with no manual logging by busy kitchen staff. That turns an invisible operational habit into clear, real\-time financial data: what is being wasted, what it costs, and where to stop it. The results are real. Kitchens using Positive Carbon have cut the value of the food they waste by up to 90%, lifting gross profit by 2–8% and returning several times what they spend with us. We work with hospitality groups, universities and workplace caterers across Ireland, the UK and the DACH region And we are still small. That is the opportunity. The people we hire now will shape the product, the culture and the company with real ownership, a direct line to the founder, and a mission where, unusually, the economics and the planet pull in exactly the same direction. We're hiring our first in\-house engineer. Until now our platform has been built with an external development partner; you'll bring it in\-house, own it, and set the technical direction from here. This is a founding role in the real sense your decisions on architecture, tooling and engineering culture will stick. Roughly a third of all the food the world produces is thrown away, and after households, the kitchens that feed us in hotels, offices, hospitals and universities are among the biggest wasters of all. A single commercial kitchen can bin tens of thousands of euro of food a year without ever seeing it on a balance sheet. It is one of the largest, most solvable problems in climate today, and almost nobody can measure it. Positive Carbon makes it measurable. Founded in Dublin in 2020, we build AI that sees what a kitchen throws away. Our sensor sits above any bin and uses computer vision, the same technology found in self\-driving cars, to automatically capture the weight, volume and type of every item discarded, with no manual logging by busy kitchen staff. That turns an invisible operational habit into clear, real\-time financial data: what is being wasted, what it costs, and where to stop it. The results are real. Kitchens using Positive Carbon have cut the value of the food they waste by up to 90%, lifting gross profit by 2–8% and returning several times what they spend with us. We work with hospitality groups, universities and workplace caterers across Ireland, the UK and the DACH region And we are still small. That is the opportunity. The people we hire now will shape the product, the culture and the company with real ownership, a direct line to the founder, and a mission where, unusually, the economics and the planet pull in exactly the same direction. Tasks * Own the Positive Carbon platform end to end: the food\-waste capture pipeline, the data and analytics layer, and the dashboards customers use to find and reduce wasted\-food cost. * Turn raw measurement into financial insight waste value by site, category and meal period. * Build AI into the product as a first\-class capability, not a bolt\-on: agents and LLM pipelines for enrichment, reporting, anomaly detection and operational recommendations. * Use AI aggressively in how you build. We expect a small team to ship like a much larger one. * Stand up the engineering foundations we'll need to scale: CI/CD, observability, data quality and a sane release process. * Transition us cleanly off the agency setup without disrupting live customers. Requirements * Startup or founding experience (must\-have): you've worked at an early\-stage company or in a founding/first\-hire role and you're at home with ambiguity, ownership and a blank page. * Strong full\-stack experience across backend, frontend and data and the instinct to go deep wherever the hard problem is. * A track record of shipping production software that real customers rely on. * Genuine fluency with modern AI tooling you build with LLMs and agents and have opinions on where they help and where they don't. * Pragmatism over perfection: you scope hard, ship, and iterate. * Comfort being the technical owner when there's no one else to ask. Benefits * Health Insurance * Pension * Bike to work * 25 Days Annual Leave * Hybrid Work * Computer vision, IoT/edge or data\-pipeline experience. * Experience as a founding engineer or very early hire. * Interest in the climate and food\-systems problem. **Send a short note on why this role, plus a CV or LinkedIn, or through our website. Tell us about something you built, shipped or closed not just where you worked.**

description: LotusWorks is an Engineering Services provider specializing in managing Commissioning, Construction Services, Calibration, Operations \& Maintenance scopes of work within global manufacturing facilities. As a Graduate Electrical Engineer (Commissioning), you will support the Electrical Commissioning Team in the execution of electrical commissioning activities on Data Centre projects across Europe. Working alongside experienced engineers and technicians, you will receive structured training, mentoring, and field exposure to build competence in commissioning activities from Level 2 (Installation Verification) through to Level 5 (Integrated System Testing). Travel to project sites across Europe for extended periods is a core requirement of this position. **Responsibilities** * Support electrical commissioning activities across Data Centre projects under the guidance of experienced commissioning personnel. * Participate in electrical design reviews and assist in identifying and documenting design discrepancies. * Review project specifications, control philosophies, System of Operation (SOO), Description of Operation (DOO), and equipment submittals. * Read and interpret electrical drawings, including single\-line diagrams, schematics, and layouts. * Assist in the development and review of commissioning test scripts and procedures. * Witness and support Level 2 to Level 5 commissioning activities, including Installation Verification Checks (IVC), Pre\-Functional Checks (PFC), Functional Performance Tests (FPT), and Integrated System Tests (IST). * Execute assigned commissioning activities once trained and authorised by the Electrical Lead. * project meetings and provide status updates on assigned activities. * Support the review of commissioning documentation and document management systems. * Any other duties or responsibilities that may be assigned to you in your role. **Skills Required** * Strong interest in electrical engineering and commissioning within mission\-critical environments. * Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, schematics, and engineering documentation. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent attention to detail and organisational abilities. * Effective written and verbal communication skills. * Ability to work both independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. * Willingness to learn and develop technical knowledge through training and practical experience. * Ability to travel extensively across Europe for project assignments. * Commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality. **Education / Licenses / Certifications** * Bachelor's Degree in Electrical Engineering or a related engineering discipline. * Recent graduates or candidates with relevant internship/work placement experience are encouraged to apply. * Full driving licence preferred. * Proof of education, licenses, and certifications will be required where applicable **Benefits** * VHI Healthcare Cover * Life Assurance * Pension Contribution * Training And Educational Assistance * Annual GP Check\-Up * Extra Annual Leave * Healthcare Screening * Recognition Rewards *LotusWorks is an equal opportunity employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where all employees feel valued, respected, and empowered.*

title: Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer
description: **Role Purpose** The role of a Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer is to execute engineering and design activities of the project. To conduct design reviews, project engineering, commissioning support ensuring consistent application of agreed procedures and utilization of standard forms. Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer’s Roles \& Responsibilities **General** * Assist and conduct the mechanical engineering and design activities on the project. * Support mechanical package owners. * Ensure the design management process is being followed on the project by either taking on the role directly. **EHS** * Read and understand EHS Plan. * Read, understand and promote the mechanical safe systems of work (MSSW) **Procurement** * Assist in the production of pre\-qualification packs (PQP) for design services and RFQ’s for equipment. Forward to project quantity surveyor for Subcontractor Package or Procurement Manager for Purchases. **RFQ Shall Include** * Scope of Works * Drawings and Specifications * Compliance, Deviation and Exclusion Matrices * Schedule \- deliveries, installation and commissioning requirements PQQ shall be in line with MP17 \- Selection and appointment of design consultants. * Mark\-up equipment and design services quotes to advise the project manager of the correct scope that aligns with the technical submittal and design brief. * Develop and update procurement \& design schedule/tracker with project planner. Continuously update the tracker until all technical submittals achieve status A, design deliverables accepted and materials/equipment have been delivered. **Quality** * Be engaged and utilize the documentation workflow for the project. Documentation generation, peer review, submission, re\-submission, distribution etc. * Review, comment and understand Quality Plan. * Assist in the development the project scope specific ITP considering project specification, client and local requirements. * Assist in the development of system tracker including equipment detail, interfacing details (electrical \& control), commissioning checklists and walkdown requirements * Review and understand any local authority requirements. * Develop lessons learned log of the project and continually update. * Indicate what the learning was * What the outcome or mitigations were * Indicate whether it was an internal finding or should be reported externally * Indicate if it was a cost, time or both impacts. * Assist in the O\&Ms production in line with the specification and agreed program. **Planning** * Understand and provide input and updates to the project schedule. * Input ROJ dates of CFCI equipment onto the equipment tracker. * Request and monitor the OFCI delivery dates. **Engineering \& Design** * Complete familiarisation and technical reviews on projects and record notes. Pose questions to the estimators and submit relevant RFI’s for clarifications on design/ scope. * Assist in the development of systems tracker along with equipment tag references, details and interfaces. * Assist in the development and tracking of design responsibility matrix/ contractor design portion (CDP). * Assist with the development and input into Design Deviation and Exclusions Log to ensure the team are aware of the agreed deviations and exclusions from design. * Validate the design using the request for information (RFI), design review process and performing the appropriate high level technical calculations. * Assist in the development of schematic’s when gaps exist in the design. * Review and understand The BIM execution plan, the Commissioning Plan and the Tracking Plan for the project. * Review the federated BIM model. * Review the coordinated drawings for construction to ensure it is in compliance with the P\&ID’s and schematics and that there is sufficient access to plant and equipment for commissioning, maintenance and replacement. * Development and track Technical Submittals until Status A. * Assist in the management of change from a technical perspective, updating change logs and schedules as required. * Tag Devices and instrumentation, spot each device on site in consultation with controls vendor and designer. * Attend biweekly technical meetings with members of the design team, subcontractors and internal project engineering team. * Assist in the verification of pipe and pump, duct and fan sizing and ensure design selections are suitable. * Assist in the production of the O\&M manuals in compliance with the specification. * Execute the project design and engineering process to ensure compliance. * Assist in technical review of tenders when required. * Attend and contribute to engineering and design kick\-off meeting at the outset of the project and record the minutes when required. * Attend and contribute to weekly engineering and design meetings and record the minutes when required. * Own action items identified during meetings and close them in a timely manner. **Communication** * Contribute to the development of weekly engineering and design reports and trackers. * Populate the project risk log and highlight any risks that arise to the project manager. * Manage and resolve risks and potential roadblocks with mitigation and management plans. **Commercial** * Understand the commercial fundamentals of a project including, budgets, material substation. * Understand commercial impacts of potential variation and assist the quantity surveyor in compiling variations. * Advise the project quantity surveyor and the project manager of any potential cost impacts to the project on an ongoing basis. **HR** * Attend CPD workshops and events. * Proactively and vigilantly keep information secure and fully comply with the Kirby Information Security Management System (ISMS).

title: Graduate Program
