title: Graduate Sales Executive (Greek Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English and Greek * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: HR Executive (Fixed Term Contract)
description: **HR Executive** ***12 month Fixed Term Contract*** ***Our vision is to make net zero a reality by solving the commercial mechanics*** ***of a decarbonised energy system.*** ElectroRoute is an international, renewables focused, energy trading and services company. The company employs just under 100 energy professionals based in Ireland, the UK and Europe and is a subsidiary of Mitsubishi Corporation. ElectroRoute intelligently leverages the benefits of new technologies, our advanced methodologies, and the application of unrivalled expertise to develop innovative energy trading and renewable risk management solutions. These mitigate the factors which impede clean energy investment and help make net zero a reality. Our expert trading team operates 24x7 in Ireland, the UK, across Continental Europe and in Japan; utilising our bespoke trading platform, ElectroRoute CORE, to provide essential trading services to wind farms, solar farms, and batteries. The company is a significant commodity trader on its own account – trading a range of energy commodities including Power and Gas, in the global energy markets with activities spanning Europe to Japan. Our ambitious growth plans will see us increasing the volume and variety of our activities over the coming years, requiring growth in the strength and depth of our team to help us make net zero a reality. ElectroRoute is seeking to appoint an HR Executive on a 12\-month fixed\-term contract to provide maternity cover within the HR team. The successful candidate will be responsible for payroll and benefits administration, while also delivering broad HR support across the organisation. **The Position** **Payroll Processing / Benefit Management** * You will be responsible for the accurate, timely, and compliant delivery of payroll across designated countries or entities, acting as a key payroll specialist who will own end\-to\-end payroll processing which iincludes the review and validation of payroll inputs and outputs, coordination of payroll calendars and statutory deadlines and the processing of all payroll changes such as new hires, terminations, salary changes, bonuses, benefits, leaves. * Prepare and validate payroll submissions for our payroll providers * Completing salary analysis and preparing journals for finance. * Ensure full compliance with payroll legislation, including tax, pensions, BIK, and other statutory obligations * Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date payroll and employee records on payroll and HRIS. * Respond to payroll queries from employees, providing clear explanations and timely resolutions * Work closely with HR and Finance teams to ensure seamless payroll integration and data accuracy * Carry out payroll audits and investigate and resolve discrepancies * Administration, reconciliation and payment of third\-party payroll deductions to relevant pension and healthcare providers * Completion of forms relating to Department of Social Protection and mortgage applications **HR Executive** * Support and promote the company culture by championing organisational values across the business. * Respond promptly and professionally to employee HR queries. * Coordinate recruitment activities for internship, temporary, and permanent roles. * Support the onboarding process for new hires, including contract preparation and ensuring a smooth transition into the organisation. * Administer employee benefits, including healthcare, pension, and related schemes. * Prepare and issue employee documentation across all stages of the employment lifecycle. * Maintain accurate HR records, reporting, and documentation within BambooHR. * Provide general HR administrative support, including employee letters and document management. * Coordinate internal company communications and HR\-related sessions as required. * Manage recruitment administration, including job advertising, agency coordination, interview scheduling, and interview support. * Support employees with visa and work permit administration where required. * Assist in delivering employee wellbeing initiatives and engagement activities. * Deliver induction training, performance management training, and other HR training programmes. * Manage relationships with external HR vendors and service providers. * Work collaboratively with the HR team to foster employee engagement, team spirit, and a positive workplace culture, including supporting company events and celebrations. **The Candidate** * Minimum of 3 – 5 years HR or payroll experience * Third level qualification in Payroll, HR, Business or related discipline is desirable. * Good planning and organisational skills * High level of accuracy and exceptional attention to detail * Ability to work effectively under pressure and meet strict deadlines * Proficient in MS Office, particularly Excel * Strong administrative and document management skills * Use of Bamboo system or other HRIS * Flexible and eager to learn material in new fields and develop new work practices * Ability to work independently while contributing positively as part of a team **Personal Attributes** * **Proactive and Independent** : Ability to take initiative and work autonomously while managing various tasks. * **Professionalism** : A professional attitude and approach, both internally with colleagues and externally with clients and vendors. * **Adaptability** : Flexibility to adapt to changing environments and manage unforeseen challenges. * **Confidentiality** : Ability to handle sensitive information discreetly and professionally. ***The role will be based in Dublin and will be hybrid (3 days in the office)*** ElectroRoute is an equal opportunity employer. We aim to create value through diversity and inclusion. We seek to attract candidates to reflect the diverse nature of our society. Diversity and inclusion allow the ElectroRoute team to be curious, creative, and promote positive change. We understand the many demands on all our employees, and we adopt flexible working arrangements where possible. Working at ElectroRoute offers something special for everyone.

title: HVAC Utilities Engineer
description: **Location** Dublin, Leinster, Ireland **Job ID** R\-239533 **Date posted** 02/06/2026 **This is what you will do:** The HVAC Utilities Engineer is responsible for ensuring that all site HVAC utilities systems and controlled Temperature Units perform predictably and continue to operate as required in order to support the manufacturing operations process. The HVAC Utilities Engineer also ensures that all utility systems conform to relevant regulatory requirements as well as company specifications and requirements. The incumbent will also support the implementation of process improvements and enhancements. This includes scope definition, design, change control and project management while partnering with Quality and advancing projects through the relevant Alexion quality systems. **You will be responsible for:** * Be a system matter expert (SME) for all HVAC Utilities and cleanroom cGMP systems across the site * Primary Point of Contact for HVAC Utility Projects across site. * Participate on capital project teams through all phases of projects; these phases will include conceptual design, detailed design, equipment procurement, construction, installation, start up, commissioning, and qualification. Review of design documents, commissioning documents, FAT, SAT, SDS documents, contractor protocols. Attending FATs as required. Inspection of and acceptance of utility / HVAC installation. * Support validation with Controlled Temperature Unit document reviews and investigations. * Support the Site Wide Energy and Sustainability Program * Generate and/or update Standard Operating Procedures or engineering work instructions as required. * Responsible for initiation and completion of deviations, CAPAs, and change controls for utility systems * Provide support to manufacturing and utility operations where required. * Ensure adherence to and completion of preventative maintenance program. HVAC Utilities Engineer Location: ADMF, College Park Global Level: D Reports To: Darren O’Malley, Utilities Manager * Ensure that new equipment is included in the plant maintenance and calibration program. * Plans and manages maintenance and shutdown activities. * Performs investigations relating to system / equipment malfunctions. * Provide engineering and technical support to enhance reliability of Utilities HVAC. * SME support for internal and external regulatory audits. * Play a primary role in continuous improvement of the site Utilities Programme to ensure compliance with all global, site and regulatory requirements. * Perform additional duties at the request of the direct supervisor. **You will need to have:** * Multi\-disciplined experience in mechanical/electrical/controls. * A minimum of 5 years' experience working in an Engineering or Utilities / Facilities project role. * A minimum of 5 Years cGMP industrial experience. * Understanding and practical knowledge of Site HVAC Systems. * Understanding and practical knowledge of CTU’s * Experience in implementing root cause analysis and effective investigation practices in an Operational Excellence environment. * Ability to keep work pace and meet deadlines. Good organising and planning skills. * Demonstrated proficiency communicating and collaborating at a variety of levels with customers, vendors, equipment suppliers and operations staff. * Hands on technical experience in Utilities / facilities systems and building management systems. * Ability to communicate effectively with Cross Functional Stakeholders **We would prefer for you to have:** * An engineering degree or equivalent relevant engineering experience. **Date Posted** 03\-Jun\-2026 **Closing Date** 16\-Jun\-2026 Our mission is to build an inclusive and equitable environment. We want people to feel they belong at AstraZeneca and Alexion, starting with our recruitment process. We welcome and consider applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of characteristics. We offer reasonable adjustments/accommodations to help all candidates to perform at their best. If you have a need for any adjustments/accommodations, please complete the section in the application form.

title: Health & Safety Assistant (Graduate Position)
description: **ABOUT AMERICAN FRUITS AND FLAVORS** American Fruits and Flavors Ireland, DAC, (AFFI) has made its mark in the industry by processing the finest fruit juices, fruit concentrates, juice blends and spray dried powder versions of hundreds of unique fruit and flavor combinations for the food and beverage industry over 50 years. **POSITION OVERVIEW** Support with the development /collation and administration of programs and policies that result in Plant\-wide compliance with Health \& Safety statutory requirements. The position supports the safety function to create and maintain a safe and healthy working environment and assists the EHS coordinator with the management \& delivery of the Health \& Safety agenda and programmes for the site. Must contribute to the efficient and profitable operation of the Athy plant and maintain a positive work atmosphere, behaving and communicating in ways that foster good relationships with employees, co\-workers and managers, while achieving performance standards. **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES \& DUTIES** 1\. Under the supervision and guidance of the EHS Coordinator: 1\.1\. Maintain the company’s Health \& Safety Management System 1\.2\. Assessing chemical handling and risk assessing SDS for hazardous materials (raw materials and batches). Integration of chemical controls and requirements into emergency plan –procedures \& identification of health surveillance requirements. 1\.3\. Collating chemical register and ensuring SDS are available and in the system for all products onsite. 1\.4\. Review and develop safety documents for assigned tasks including Policies, SOPs, forms and internal audit check sheets and reviews. 1\.5\. Prepare and or review process risk assessments of site operations. 1\.6\. Assist with employee work\-related injury investigation and ensure that the corrective action is completed. 1\.7\. Assist with the delivery of new starter inductions and contractor induction training 1\.8\. Assist with the continual development and roll out of H\&S training to site personnel. 1\.9\. Promote a positive safety culture. 2\. Perform safety inspections and audits and behavioral observations of work areas, documentation and files. 3\. Assist in organizing and completing fire and emergency preparedness drills. 4\. Maintain and co\-ordinate PPE inventory, issue and stocks management. 5\. Maintain and review day\-to\-day safety records including first aid supplies, emergency check sheets and contractors risk management systems. 6\. Monitor, analyse, and report on H\&S performance metrics, highlighting trends and improvement opportunities. 7\. Co\-ordination of safety training programme: (mandatory training) sourcing suppliers, co\-ordination training, managing records. 8\. Co\-ordinating PPE issue and inventory. 9\. Managing first aid provisions and systems. 10\. Determine appropriate and effective safety and compliance signage, bulletins and safety updates as required. 11\. Review contractor’s safety induction, risk assessments and safety compliance 12\. Maintain familiarity with company policies and procedures pertaining to Quality Manual, Food Security/Food Defense, Sanitation, cGMP's, and HACCP/Food Safety. 13\. Other duties and special projects as assigned. ***This list is for guidance only and the post holder will be required to undertake additional duties in line with the changing needs of the business.*** Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * On\-site parking Application question(s): * Do you have a degree in Health \& Safety or a related field? Work Location: In person

title: I&A General Operative
description: **Department:** I\&A Processing **Location:** Portlaoise **Description** At *Enva* we are committed to Recycling and Resource Recovery; it forms the basis of our business strategy and our values. We recover waste products to provide either a second life, such as the production of energy or, in many cases, full closed\-loop recycling solutions. We also provide a complete portfolio of water and wastewater services. Our dedication to developing new and innovative products and solutions and extending the lifecycle of the world's resources is driving our business forward, saving energy and saving resources. The General Operative role is to assist in all areas of the plant as and when required and to deal with the acceptance and processing of waste and exports in compliance with all legislative and administrative requirements, and in line with our EPA licence. **Key Responsibilities** • To carry out all required general operative duties which may include, but which will not be limited to: • incoming waste (e.g. paint waste) is properly tracked and segregated into the appropriate waste streams and to account for outgoing waste accordingly • processing of fuel oil as required • operation of filter press as required • cleaning of tanks (confined space work) as required • loading and unloading of vehicles as required • additional services developed or offered by the company • To conduct your duties in accordance with company standard operating procedures (SOPs) and the company’s waste licence • To complete all paperwork and administration necessary as requested by your supervisor or as the business needs dictate • To adhere to the company’s Health and Safety policy and procedures at all times, including but not limited to: • To wear the appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (P.P.E.) at all times • To highlight any safety concerns to your Manager immediately and to report any accidents/ incidents to your Manager immediately • To report all non\-conformances to the relevant personnel and to follow up to ensure compliance requirements are met • To ensure that housekeeping is kept to a high standard around the site and that all activities comply with health and safety requirements • To communicate any issues that arise to the Operations Manager/ Supervisor immediately • To attend plant meetings, company meetings, training courses etc. as required • To provide direct operational support to any and all areas of the business as required and as reasonably requested by the company including but not limited to support for off site operations (e.g. waste oil and garage services, field services) • To be flexible to carry out other duties as may be requested of your Manager from time to time • To communicate any issues that arise within the plant/ process to the Operations Manager or next most appropriate person immediately • To maintain vehicles, work equipment, materials and PPE clean and in working order and to report any loss or damage immediately • To carry out checks , inspections and audits as directed \#ijadvanatge **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** • To act courteously and communicate effectively with customers, suppliers, Enva personnel and other personnel encountered within your role; • To foster and develop good customer and public relations at all times and to portray a professional image of the company; • To report for work in a timely manner and to conduct work as scheduled to company and customer satisfaction; • To work within the instructions and procedures of the Company’s systems as directed by your Supervisor or other management as appropriate • To conduct all duties in a constructive, efficient and productive manner at all times; • To act diligently and professionally in the interests of the Company; • To report any personal circumstances that would affect your ability to legally perform your duties. ***The above description is a general outline of the requirements of the role. It is not intended to be exhaustive and may be added to in the future depending on the needs of the business.*** **Why Work for Enva?** Enva is committed to providing more than just jobs, we want to grow our business by offering career paths that match the aspirations of the talent that is increasingly aware of the need to protect the planet we all share. For the right candidates, the possibilities are truly endless and we will offer all the support, training and benefits you need to help you realise your true potential. Examples of Company Benefits: * A competitive salary in a growing company committed to our people. * A competitive holiday allowance. * Company pension scheme. (if applicable) * Enhanced family friendly policies. * Employee Assistance Program * Employee Life Insurance. * Bike2Work scheme. * Free on\-site parking. ***The successful candidate will be required to complete a pre employment medical assessment, including drug and alcohol testing as part of the recruitment process.***

title: IT Service Management Analyst
description: **BASIC PURPOSE:** The Purpose of the IT Service Management Analyst is to Provide full range of reporting on IT services supplied to the business. Ensure that major service issues are recorded, tracked and (in conjunction with the dept. manager) addressed effectively across the local and regional organisation. **PRINCIPAL ACCOUNTABILITIES:** The principal accountabilities of the role will be: * Provide effective and timely reporting related to service level attainment * Major issues * Outages and escalations * Initiate escalations if/when required to vendors (ITS, HP. BT etc.) * Help to determine the root cause of issues, liaising with required support teams across the IT organisation * Use service reporting to identify trends and highlight positive movements or the need for action to improve service performance * Be customer service focused at all times in delivering IT services to the business **Skills / Qualifications:** * Leaving Cert Level * Good Microsoft Office skills * Strong communication (written and oral) * Good organisational skills and ability to work on own initiative and within a team critical * ITIL Foundation beneficial * Ability to upskill

description: **Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer – MSP** **South County Dublin \| 5 days office\-based \| MSP experience required** A small, well\-established MSP in **South County Dublin** is hiring a **Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer** to join its service desk team. This role is for someone with around **2–3 years’ IT support experience gained in an MSP environment** . MSP experience is required, as you will be supporting multiple clients, managing ticket flow, switching between different environments, and troubleshooting at pace. This is a **5 days per week office\-based role** . This is non\-negotiable. You must already be **living in South County Dublin** , and you must be **fully legal to work in Ireland now** . There is **no visa sponsorship and no relocation support** for this role. Most of the work will be service desk based, supporting clients remotely, but there may be occasional site visits when needed. This is not a field engineer role, but you will need to be presentable, client\-focused, and comfortable visiting client sites from time to time. **What you’ll be doing** * Managing and resolving support tickets across multiple clients * Troubleshooting Windows desktop, user, application and connectivity issues * Supporting Microsoft 365, Entra ID / Active Directory and user account issues * Helping users with MFA, Authenticator apps and access\-related problems * Basic network troubleshooting across TCP/IP, DNS and related issues * Supporting security tools and processes such as EDR, MDR and general cyber security hygiene * Communicating clearly with clients by phone, email and ticket updates * Escalating issues properly when needed * Carrying out occasional client site visits when required **What you’ll need** * Around **2–3 years’ IT support experience** * Previous **MSP experience** — this is required * Strong Level 1 support ability, with some Level 2 troubleshooting experience * Good Windows troubleshooting experience * Good understanding of TCP/IP, DNS and basic networking * Experience with Active Directory and/or Entra ID * Exposure to Microsoft 365 * Awareness of MFA, Authenticator apps, EDR/MDR and general cyber security basics * Strong written and verbal communication skills * A positive, can\-do attitude and willingness to keep learning * Good troubleshooting instincts and the ability to move quickly between issues * You must be living in **South County Dublin** * Access to your own car or motorbike would be very useful, but it is not essential This role would suit someone who enjoys the variety of MSP work, wants exposure to different clients and technologies, and is happy working in a small, flexible team. Salary for this Level 1 Technical Support Engineer role ranges from €35,000 \- €39,000 depending on your experience. Apply with your CV if you experience as a Technical Support Engineer and your location fits the requirements.

title: IT/OT Telecoms Network Architect
description: We are the people behind Ireland’s electricity grid. We operate the power system with world\-leading levels of variable asynchronous renewable generation, ensuring that everyone has power when they need it. But that’s not all — we are now transforming the power system for future generations. Our purpose is clear: to enable society to reduce carbon emissions and achieve climate change and net\-zero ambitions. This transformation requires innovation, resilience, and leadership in designing and deploying next\-generation OT and IT architectures that underpin critical infrastructure. **The Team** EirGrid Group has a unique role to play in leading this radical transformation of our power system in support of the transition to renewable energy. This will require pioneering engineering effort together with enhanced IT capability to respond successfully to the scale of disruption. This offers a unique opportunity to further your career in Telecom and Network Architecture while playing a significant role in delivering EirGrid Group’s Infrastructure transformation. We’re a Networking intensive business, and we operate a state\-of\-the\-art, specialised business\-critical systems, 24/7\. **The Opportunity** We are seeking an experienced **Network Architect** to join our growing team and play a key role in shaping the future of Ireland’s energy infrastructure. As we transition to a low\-carbon energy future, this is a unique opportunity to operate at a strategic level, influencing network architecture for both current operations and future requirements, including new interconnector projects and offshore renewable deployments. The Network Architect will be pivotal in designing, implementing, and governing the telecommunications and network architecture that underpins EirGrid’s critical operations. With ambitious plans to deploy offshore renewable energy generators over the next five years, there is an urgent need to enhance Network capabilities to support telemetry, SCADA, and Corporate assets and associated services for these installations. **Key Responsibilities*** Define and maintain the OT/IT network architecture roadmap, ensuring alignment with organisational strategy and regulatory requirements. * Lead the design and deployment of secure, high\-performance network solutions for critical infrastructure, including offshore renewable projects and interconnectors. * Oversee integration of OT and IT systems, ensuring interoperability, resilience, and compliance with cybersecurity standards. * Collaborate with offshore teams and external partners to deliver consistent architecture standards and accelerate project timelines. * Provide technical leadership and governance across network design, implementation, and operational support. * Develop and enforce architecture principles, policies, and best practices, including automation and standardisation. * Support business continuity and disaster recovery planning for network\-related services. * Act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, influencing decisions on technology investments and solution approaches. **About You*** A recognised qualification in a relevant technical discipline supported by 10 years’ experience in senior network engineering or architecture roles within critical infrastructure environments. * Expert Knowledge of CISCO product suite Nexus, Catalyst, especially switching and routing * Proven expertise in OT networking, routing, switching, and WAN technologies (including MPLS) Port channels, VPC’s, Physical infrastructure. * Strong knowledge of network security, vulnerability management, and remediation. * Experience in architecting and delivering complex network solutions, including cloud and hybrid environments. * Demonstrated ability to develop operational procedures and run books, with a focus on automation. * Excellent technical and analytical skills, with the ability to operate effectively in complex environments. * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate across teams. Desirable * Professional certifications such as CCNP, CCIE, Azure Network Engineer Associate, or equivalent. * Experience with network automation tools and scripting (e.g., Python, Ansible, Netbox, OpsManage\+). **Closing date for applications** 08/06/2026 Please note we receive a high volume of applications, and as every application is carefully reviewed our response time may vary. If you agree to be considered for other roles this may result in a delayed response as we establish the best fit for your experience. We appreciate your patience. We value potential as much as experience**.** If you believe you can succeed in this role, we encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every requirement. We're dedicated to diversity and inclusion at EirGrid. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and the importance of a supportive culture for all our people to achieve their potential. Our diversity and inclusion networks have been designed to create an inclusive culture that enables our people to feel a sense of belonging at work. As an equal opportunities’ employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process.

title: InfoSec Analyst I
description: MongoDB’s Security Assurance team is on a mission to make MongoDB one of the world’s most trusted technology vendors. We are doing this in two ways. First, we provide customers with the clear, concise and accurate information about the security of our products that customers require to use our products. Second, we serve as trusted security SMEs to MongoDB’s field teams. The InfoSec Analyst I is an independent contributor role. This role is vital to the Security Assurance team’s mission and is responsible for coordinating completion of customer due diligence reviews as well as helping MongoDB’s field personnel answer complex customer security questions. We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model. Responsibilities: * Actively manage the Assurance team’s queue to ensure requests are handled promptly * Complete customer security reviews. Help other team members complete reviews as necessary * Help customer facing personnel respond to customer security questions * Monitor relevant channels and answer in scope questions when possible, or escalate to more senior team members * Proactively maintain the team’s knowledgebase * Proactively recommend improvements in team processes and tooling to team leadership * Share relevant and actionable customer feedback with team leadership * Help coordinate development of customer facing response to high\-profile supply chain events including critical severity CVEs that may affect MongoDB or its vendors * Lead complex projects to help build, scale and train the team * Serve as an internal subject matter expert on MongoDB product security topics, assurance trends, customer concerns, and emerging regulations * Design and deliver internal enablement training on security posture and compliance topics for teams across the organization Requirements: * 2 to 3 years of experience in Information Security * BS in Computer Science, Information Systems, or equivalent experience * Direct exposure to at least one major framework or regulation such as ISO 27001, SOC2, CSA or DORA is preferred * Basic understanding of public cloud technologies including IAM, encryption, network security and tenant isolation * Strong communication skills, with the ability to present security concepts to technical and non\-technical stakeholders up to and including at the executive levels * Ability to coordinate and manage multiple urgent requests simultaneously while effectively collaborating with SMEs across various time zones * A self\-starter with a strong desire to grow into a trusted authority on the security of MongoDB and its products Success in this Role Means: * Within 3 months \- Have a solid understanding of MongoDB’s product portfolio, core security posture, and the Security Assurance team’s procedures and tooling; reliably triage incoming requests; independently complete customer security reviews with guidance from more senior team members; monitor key internal channels and answer straightforward in‑scope questions * Within 6 months \- Independently own end‑to‑end completion of most standard customer security reviews; serve as a trusted partner to customer‑facing teams by helping them respond to complex security questions; make measurable contributions to the team knowledgebase and playbooks; proactively identify and help implement improvements to team processes and tooling * Within 12 months \- Be recognized as a trusted subject matter expert on MongoDB’s product portfolio, security posture and common customer concerns; complete complex projects that materially improve review quality, throughput, or customer experience; consistently surface relevant, actionable customer feedback that can influence Security roadmaps **About MongoDB** MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the data platformbase for the AI era, enabling buildersinnovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed data platformbase on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud\-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi\-cloud data platformbase and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. With offices worldwide and over 670,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI\-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software. Our compass at MongoDB is our Leadership Commitment, guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB. To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys. Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB, and help us make an impact on the world! MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter. MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer. *Req ID: 2273456141*

title: Integration Engineer (Client facing) Professional Services
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team Stripe’s Professional Services team works with our most strategic users. You will work on the highest visibility projects to provide technical expertise to our customers to help accelerate their knowledge, solution design and integrations with Stripe. This role is responsible for leading strategic conversations and developing plans that will transform the payments and financial infrastructure for leading enterprises. What you’ll do Responsibilities \* Plan and execute integration engineering end\-to\-end, from high\-level architecture down to code implementation. This includes conducting discovery sessions with users to understand their goals, parsing out specific use cases, and guiding users to implement high complexity integrations. * Think on your feet and communicate technical concepts, explaining how Stripe works from both a product feature and API architecture perspective. Manage user expectations on timelines and product changes. * Be the primary, ongoing technical point of contact for our largest users' and partners' technical teams. You will be responsible for resolving user integration issues and owning escalations for operational issues and priority requests. * Collaborate with Sales, Solution Architecture and Implementation teams to drive client engagement, customer wins, and revenue on behalf of the Professional Services team * Collaborate closely with Product and Engineering to proactively identify potential enhancements and new features based on feedback you’ll gather from the field. * Provide deep technical support and debugging, inspecting and understanding the Stripe codebase and thoroughly testing functionality to best advise users, while also building and demonstrating proof\-of\-concept integrations, creating user\-specific test plans, and answering ad\-hoc technical questions. * In some cases, lead "Hands On Keyboard" implementations for user engagements, providing material feedback on coding practices, CI/CD, and software architecture. * Review and optimize existing user integrations and provide recommendations to improve performance, reliability, and efficiency. * Build tools and analytics; create dashboards, run queries, and generate custom data reports to support user business reviews and operational needs. * Develop and share knowledge, building reusable processes to enable the larger Professional Services team. Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. Minimum requirements \* 4 years \+ experience in a Software Engineering / Integration Engineering role. * Ability to write code (in programming languages like Go, Java, C/C\+\+ etc.) and quickly debug user code snippets. * Comfortable with various code\-level debugging (Stripe code and user code) and using code as a tool for problem\-solving. * Strong generalist technical experience, including proficiency in SQL and an understanding of data structures and algorithms. * Understanding of the payments domain and API security topics. * Be comfortable talking about technical concepts, and explaining how Stripe works, both from a product features and API architecture perspective. * A user first approach \& empathy with our users, capable of mapping business requirements into reliable technical solutions. * Confident and comfortable with customers, with the ability to lead strategic technical conversations. Preferred requirements \* Enjoy the puzzle of solving open\-ended problems, both individually and as a member of our team. Willingness to learn and teach others, including mentoring newer team members. * Collaborative, communicative, consultative; able to apply specific methodologies to ensure repeatability of engagements. * Intellectually curious, with great problem solving skills. * Technical writing skills to document complex integration guidance and processes. In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €104,000 \- €156,000\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programmes as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success. Our capabilities include programme, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions. We are majority\-owned by CBRE Group, Inc., the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, with our partners holding a significant minority interest. Turner \& Townsend and CBRE work together to provide clients with the premier programme, project and cost management offering in markets around the world. Please visit our website: www.turnerandtownsend.com **Job Description** The Junior Contract Administrator is responsible for providing support to the Commercial Administration Lead regarding the management and administration of contracts within the client organisation. The role holder is responsible for the day\-to\-day administrative tasks related to contract management, including drafting, reviewing and managing contracts. The Junior Contract Administrator ensures all contracts comply with legal and organisational guidelines. The role holder prepares and organises contract related documents, maintains record and supports contract negotiations. **Principal Accountabilities** * Assist in drafting, reviewing, and finalising of all contracts, ensuring that all terms and conditions are clear and comply with the client organisations polices and legal requirements. * Maintain organised and up\-to\-date records of all contracts and related documents, ensuring that all contract documentation is stored securely and is easily accessible. * Identify, assess, and mitigate risks associated with contract administration. * Serve as a point of contact for internal and external stakeholders regarding contract\-related inquiries. * Update and maintain the contract management system with accurate and current information. * Communicate contract terms and conditions to internal and external parties. * Track contract milestones, deadlines, and renewals. * Generate reports on contract status and performance, providing support during contract negotiations. * Manage contract\-related data, including contract management software or databases. * Assist with invoicing, payment processing, and other financial tasks related to contracts. * Prepare reports on contract status, performance, and compliance. * Support communication and collaboration with vendors and suppliers. * Perform additional tasks, activities or other duties as may be required or assigned as part of the role. **Experience** * 3 years’ experience in a contract administration support role in a high value rail projects or projects of a similar scale / complexity. * Experience in assisting with the drafting, reviewing, and administration of commercial contracts. * Experience dealing with a range of stakeholders at all levels across the organisation. **Skills** * Good influencing skills to manage and motivate teams in support of delivery of high risk, value and complex project and programmes. * Ability to coordinate and organise multiple sources of reporting inputs / performance data and deliver project / programme reports as required. * An ability to work effectively in a team environment. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills to facilitate clear and consistent dialogue between all parties involved. * Ability to govern and drive outcomes to ensure high quality service. * Comprehensive knowledge of cost management principles, methods, techniques and tools, including the standard method of measurement. **Qualifications** * C Eng / BEng / BA / BSc cost management, quantity surveying, estimating management or in a related discipline. **Additional Information** **What we offer you:** * Full time * Competitive remuneration and attractive range of benefits * 8% Pension * 23 days Annual leave (\+ 2 company days) * Opportunity to work on impactful and innovative projects * Career development opportunities both in Ireland and globally * Opportunity to work with a diverse group of talented and collaborative colleagues Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change. We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work\-life balance. Turner \& Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community. Join our social media conversations for more information about Turner \& Townsend and our exciting future projects: Twitter Instagram LinkedIn

title: Junior Electrical Design Engineer
description: Anord Mardix, a Flex company, is a global leader in critical power solutions. With facilities around the globe, Anord Mardix leads in critical power infrastructure, supporting a myriad of industries, from financial institutions to data centers. We develop skill sets, encourage curiosity, and enable empowerment – we turn jobs into careers. If this sounds interesting, we’d like to meet you. **Job Summary** --------------- To support our extraordinary teams who build great products and contribute to our growth, we’re looking to add a **Junior Electrical Design Engineer** located in **Dundalk, Ireland.** This role will provide excellent experience in the design and development of electrical switchgear and associated products. We are looking for a graduate level design engineer with an eye for detail who can work independently and as part of a team. Good interpersonal and communication skills are a must, as the successful candidate will work with multiple disciplines during products lifecycle. **What a typical day looks like:** * Under the direction and mentorship of senior members of the design team, carry out the electrical design of switchgear and other critical power distribution products. * Create general arrangements and electrical schematics using AutoCAD and AutoCAD electrical * Generate accurate bills of materials for purchasing along with other supporting documentation such as work instructions * Maintain accurate \& up to date records of designs and documentation * Participate in providing technical support to internal and external customers, attending meetings where necessary * Work actively within the engineering team to help foster a positive and supportive working environment **The experience we’re looking to add to our team:** * Have a third level qualification in Electrical Engineering or come from a related technical background * Relevant experience in a switchgear production environment (electrical) will be considered in lieu of a third level qualification * Have a solid understanding of electricity fundamentals and electrical circuit design * Be proficient in the use of Microsoft Office tools * Have a methodical approach to problem solving * Understanding of electrical engineering drawings such as single line diagrams and schematics is advantageous **What you’ll receive for the great work you provide:** * An environment where you will feel fulfilled by your work, valued for your contribution, and celebrated for your success; * Opportunities to learn new skills in a fast\-paced industry; * A competitive salary and benefits package that includes: + A merit\-based annual pay review + Enhanced annual leave + Employee recognition scheme and long service awards + Referral bonus + Volunteer days + Group Life Insurance, including a death\-in\-service payment and access to a virtual GP service 24/7 365 days a year + Company Pension Scheme + Sick pay scheme + Cycle to Work scheme + Enhanced maternity/paternity leave * Flexible/Remote/Hybrid Work based on your Job Function; * Travel opportunities (role dependent); * Support your well\-being by access to: + Employee Assistance Programme through VHI offering free access to qualified counsellors and expert advice. + VHI Private Health Insurance + On\-site trained Mental Health First Aiders * Access to various discount programs (including food, activities, gym memberships etc.). TH10 \#LI\-TONYHYNES **Job Category** ---------------- Production Engineering Anord Mardix is an Equal Opportunity Employer and employment selection decisions are based on merit, qualifications, and abilities. We do not discriminate based on: age, race, religion, color, sex, national origin, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, disability, pregnancy status, or any other status protected by law. We're happy to provide reasonable accommodations to those with a disability for assistance in the application process. Please email accessibility@flex.com and we'll discuss your specific situation and next steps (NOTE: this email does not accept or consider resumes or applications. This is only for disability assistance. To be considered for a position at Anord Mardix, you must complete the application process first).

title: Junior Marketing Executive
description: We're looking for a curious, driven professional who's passionate about marketing and wants to grow its strategic experience. If you're someone who spots a great shot, has a strong eye for design, and is curious to learn and ambitious to grow we are looking for you. This isn't a desk\-only role. You'll be out on\-site, capturing the work we do, and then turning that into compelling content across our channels. The role sits at the heart of the business, reporting directly to the Sales Manager and working closely with, fabrication, purchasing, and external partners. **Who We Are** Celuplast is a fast\-growing specialist supplier and fabricator of building products, serving trade markets across Ireland. We operate across trade counter, online sales, fabrication systems, and specialist product categories including landscaping, interiors, roofing, and home extension solutions. We're part of a wider group with four companies across Ireland and the UK, all focused on building products. That means there's a broader network to collaborate with, share ideas across, and for the right person, plenty of room to grow. **What You'll Be Doing** **Day\-to\-Day** • Create and manage content across our social media channels, keeping things consistent, on\-brand, and engaging • Get out on\-site to photograph and film our products, projects, and people — strong photography skills are a must • Updating the Marketing Calendar • Edit video content for use across social, web, and other channels • Coordinate with website developer so the message is consistent across product listings and landing pages. • Plan and send email marketing campaigns (at least twice a month) to trade and homeowner audiences • Support product launches with well\-timed, well\-designed assets across all channels • Collaborate with our PIM system to keep product information accurate and up to date • Create marketing collateral including brochures, flyers, presentations and digital assets. • Learn and contribute to campaign planning and execution. **What We're Looking For** **Essential** • 3\+ years of experience in design and content creation. • Strong photography skills — comfortable shooting on\-site in a real\-world, non\-studio environment • Video editing skills • Social media management experience • Experience with email marketing tools and campaigns • A genuine eye for design this is a big asset in how we evaluate candidates • Proactivity and curiosity. • Excellent Communication Skills **Nice to Have** • Google Analytics Experience • Experience working with KPIs and reporting on campaign performance • Familiarity with PIM systems or product data management • Some exposure to paid media or basic campaign strategy • Experience in a trade, construction, or building products environment • Experience in e\-commerce. **What Success Looks Like** We're not just looking for activity, we want progress. Here's how we'll measure it together: • We’ll create a plan to grow our social media following (targeting new followers per month) • Maintaining a steady organic engagement across channels • Generating leads through social media and website activity • Running email campaigns twice a month, on time and on brief • Delivering monthly reports that show ROI and conversion metrics, and knowing what to do with the data **Why Join Us?** You'll be joining a business that's growing, has a clear direction, and genuinely wants its marketing to match its ambitions. This is a role where you can shape things, not just maintain them, and build a track record that could take you into a senior position within the group. Beyond the work itself, we're a team that looks after each other. You'll find good craic in the day\-to\-day, regular social events, and a culture that takes giving back seriously as we actively support non\-profit causes as part of who we are. You'll also get real exposure to the wider building and construction community across Ireland and the UK, expanding your professional network in an industry that rewards relationships. If you're ready to get your hands dirty, pick up a camera, and make a real difference to how a growing Irish business presents itself to the world, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Junior Marketing Executive (Graduate Role)
description: **About Unify Brands** *Unify brands* is a leading sales and marketing company, which brings together a unique portfolio of exciting consumer brands. We specialise in growing brands and delivering success for our partners. **The Role** We are looking for a motivated and creative Junior Marketing Executive to join our team on an initial 3\-month fixed\-term contract, with the possibility of extending the role based on performance and business needs. This is an exciting opportunity for a recent graduate who wants hands\-on experience across a wide range of marketing activities, from social media and email marketing to influencer campaigns and live brand activations. Working with brands like Perfect Ted, DASH water, Candy Kittens and many more. Reporting to the Marketing Manager, you will play a key role in supporting the day\-to\-day marketing efforts for both Unify Brands and the portfolio of brands we represent. **Key Responsibilities** * Manage and schedule social media content across platforms, including Instagram \& LinkedIn. * Create engaging content such as graphics, videos, and captions * Update and maintain e\-commerce websites, including product listings and promotional banners * Build and send email marketing campaigns * Work on Meta Ads with the external ads manager that runs our account * Assist with influencer outreach, gifting, and campaign coordination * Support the planning and execution of brand activations, sampling events, and pop\-ups * Assist with general marketing administration and coordination **About You** * Recent graduate in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field * Strong interest in consumer brands, digital marketing, and social media * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Highly organised with strong attention to detail * Proactive and eager to learn in a fast\-paced environment * Comfortable managing multiple projects at once * Creative mindset and enthusiasm for new ideas **Desirable Skills** * Experience with Canva, CapCut, or Adobe Creative Suite * Familiarity with email marketing platforms such as Klaviyo * Experience using Shopify or other e\-commerce platforms – not required but a plus * Understanding of influencer marketing and social media trends * Basic knowledge of analytics and reporting tools * Experience using AI tools such as Chat GPT **Why Join Us?** * Gain hands\-on experience across all areas of marketing * Work with exciting challenger brands * Opportunity to contribute directly to campaigns and activations * Potential for the role to be extended beyond the initial contract * Join a small, ambitious team where your work will have real impact Application question(s): * Are you a marketing graduate? Education: * Bachelor's (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Junior Recruitment Administrator - CPM Ireland
description: **\*\*\* 3 Month Fixed Term Contract \*\*\*** Every member of the CPM team contributes to the overall growth of our business. People are at the core of CPM’s proposition, and as a member of the Human Resources team, this role will support the delivery of a positive employee experience and contribute to a culture of continuous improvement. This is an excellent opportunity for someone at an early stage in their career to gain hands\-on experience in recruitment and HR administration within a fast\-paced environment. A positive attitude, strong attention to detail, and a willingness to learn are key to success in this role. **Role Overview** The primary focus of this role is to provide administrative and coordination support to the Recruitment function within the business. You will assist with day\-to\-day recruitment activities while ensuring adherence to internal policies and company values: **We Care, We’re Curious, and We Achieve Together**. **Key Responsibilities** * Support hiring managers and the HR team with recruitment administration * Review job adverts and documentation for accuracy, spelling, and formatting * Post job vacancies across job boards and other platforms as directed * Schedule interviews and prepare interview packs * Coordinate with interview panels to organise interview logistics * Assist with onboarding administration, including issuing contracts and induction packs * Track completion of new starter documentation and follow up where required * Maintain accurate records on recruitment systems * Provide general administrative support to the HR team and assist with ad hoc projects **Skills \& Experience** **Essential:** * Strong written and verbal communication skills * Good organisational skills and attention to detail * Proficient in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint) * Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines * Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work with different stakeholders * Proactive attitude and willingness to learn * A digital mindset, with an interest in using technology to improve efficiency **Desirable:** * Previous administrative or customer service experience * Exposure to recruitment, HR, or office environments (e.g., internship, placement, or part\-time role) **Measures of Success** * Accurate and timely completion of recruitment administration tasks * Positive feedback from hiring managers and candidates * High level of data accuracy within recruitment systems * Meeting internal timelines and service level expectations **Contract Details** * **Salary:** €26,000 (pro\-rated for contract duration) * **Working Hours:** 35 hours per week **Benefits** * Flexible working environment * Employee Assistance Program * Learning and development opportunities * Exposure to a dynamic HR and recruitment function

title: Junior Systems Analyst
description: Canada Life Europe (CLE) employs over 600 people across its Irish and German offices and serves over 500,000 customers in the German market. It focuses on pensions and individual protection products, offering a combination of unit\-linked funds, unitised\-with\-profits funds and variable annuity business within its pension products. Currently CLE is amongst the top six companies in the German broker market and its goal is to become a Top 3 Broker Provider in the market. CLE is seeking a Junior Systems Analyst to join our System Analysts’ team within Information Services (I.S.). The overall I.S. function has over 120 people in the team. **Role Overview** Canada Life Europe requires a Junior Systems Analyst to work on I.S. projects, initially on the Point of Sales Delivery team. This individual will work with the I.S. and Business teams based in Germany and Ireland, and the Canada Life Partners and Brokers based in Germany. The successful candidate will be assigned to work under the direction of the Lead Analyst. As a team member he/she will be expected to work effectively with other team members such as Systems and QA Analysts, Project Managers and Software Developers. This role is part of the I.S. Systems Analysis Team which is predominantly based in Dublin, Ireland. The successful candidate will be expected to work on programmes/projects across Germany and Ireland. **What you will help us to achieve** *The main duties and responsibilities included in this role:* * Become familiar with CLEs Point of Sales Systems and propose/implement changes as required to the systems. * Create and update technical specification documents for internal \& external use * Elicit, understand and clarify the requirements of the business stakeholders * Translate requirements into business and functional specifications * Work collaboratively with the Lead Analyst based in Ireland * Work with Systems Architects/Analysts to determine potential solutions and test them for both technical and business compatibility **What you will need to be successful in the role** *Essential skills and experience:* * 3rd level education preferably in a technical discipline, or equivalent technical experience * Knowledge of XML and XSD * Knowledge of data modelling and data formats * Ability to test and troubleshoot systems software * Understanding of recognised application/service delivery processes and methodology * Excellent communication skills * Self\-motivated with a positive and flexible attitude * Excellent team player * Ability to set and work to strict project deadlines *Desired skills and experience:* * Experience working as a Technical Analyst, ideally within the insurance industry, or a large multinational environment * Knowledge/experience of Confluence and Jira * Understanding of detailed analysis techniques with the ability to translate into comprehensive documentation which can be understood by Business stakeholders and I.S. alike. * Experience in electronic data exchange * Experience in at least one programming languages with the ability to write pseudo code * Experience working on eBusiness, Web Application and Web Services projects * Understanding of insurance business practices and processes **The Person** *The successful candidate will ideally:* * Be educated to technical degree level, or equivalent experience * Fluent in German and English * Have excellent analytical, problem\-solving, and documentation skills * Have some experience working as Technical Analyst * Have experience with XML, XSD, PDF Editors, MS Office Suite * Be a dynamic individual who is a motivated self\-starter and excellent team player * Have professional communication skills with a ‘can do’ attitude * Be customer focused and result driven The company reserves the right to draw up a shortlist as part of the selection process. Where Agency assistance is required the Canada Life Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Canada Life by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this role. Even if you don’t see yourself reflected in every job requirement listed on the posting above, we encourage you to reach out and apply if you feel you are a good fit for the role. We are committed to creating a more equitable, inclusive and diverse company, and we strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientation and life experiences to apply. Canada Life Group Services is proud to be an Equal Opportunities employer. We have created an environment of inclusion and growth to help people achieve their potential, to ensure they can bring their whole selves to work and to feel valued. We celebrate diversity of thought and perspective and want all of our employees to feel valued, respected and supported. We have built a culture that promotes equality and inclusion for all of our current and future employees and strive to ensure that our behaviours, processes and practices are aligned to this culture. If you require any accommodations during the recruitment process please contact **lifecareers@irishlife.ie** and we will be delighted to ensure you are fully supported to be your best. *CLGS supports Equal Opportunity and is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland.*
