title: Part Time Cook/Trainee Cook
description: Café Jacques is a busy seaside café overlooking the south beach in Skerries. We are a café that continues to create its menu’s by using the freshest ingredients sourced from local suppliers even growing some of our own vegetables. We aim to create a café full of character but above all, a relaxing and enjoyable atmosphere with breath\-taking views. Our cafe is open from 9am \-6pm so chef/cooking hours are based between 8:30am and 6pm. We are looking for an applicant who enjoys cooking and working with food. Experience is not essential and full training will be provided where required. **The ideal candidate will have:** \- Excellent communication skills \- Be organised and flexible \- The ability to use their own initiative and work as part of our Team \- Enjoy cooking and working with food Benefits: * Employee discount Work Location: In person

title: Part-Qualified Accountant
description: **Part\-Qualified Accountant** **Location:** Lucan **Job Type:** Full\-time **Salary:** Competitive, based on experience We are seeking a motivated **Part\-Qualified Accountant** to join our growing accountancy practice. The successful candidate will work with a diverse portfolio of small and medium\-sized businesses, providing accounting, taxation, and advisory services. This role offers exposure to a broad range of accounting functions and an opportunity to develop strong client relationships within a professional practice environment. **Key Responsibilities:** * Preparation of annual financial statements for small and medium\-sized companies in accordance with relevant accounting standards * Assisting with VAT returns, RCT Returns, and VAT compliance * Preparation and submission of Income Tax returns for sole traders, partnerships, and directors * Assisting with external Audits * Preparation and filing of Corporation Tax returns * Liaising with clients to obtain financial information and provide support on accounting queries * Assisting clients with bookkeeping and cloud accounting software where required * Supporting partners and senior staff with tax planning and advisory work * Ensuring compliance with relevant tax and statutory filing deadlines * Maintaining accurate client records and working papers * Dealing with correspondence with the Revenue Commissioners where required **Qualifications \& Experience:** * Part\-qualified accountant (e.g. ACCA, Chartered Accountants Ireland, or CPA Ireland) or other relevant qualification * Experience working in an accountancy practice environment is desirable * Strong knowledge of Irish tax and accounting regulations * Experience preparing company financial statements and tax returns * Proficiency in accounting software packages and Microsoft Excel * Experience with cloud accounting platforms (e.g. Xero, Sage, Bright) is an advantage **Skills \& Competencies:** * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Excellent attention to detail * Ability to manage multiple deadlines and clients * Effective communication and client relationship skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a team **What We Offer:** * Competitive salary based on experience * Exposure to a wide range of SME clients * Support for professional development and exams * Friendly and collaborative practice environment * Flexible working options (if applicable) Job Type: Full\-time Language: * English (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Part-time Sale Assistant - Stillorgan
description: ***Location: Stillorgan*** At O'Brien's Wine, we're passionate about what we do, and we're looking for a dynamic and enthusiastic **Part time Sales Assistant** to join our team. If you're someone who loves great wine, has a knack for customer service, and is eager to learn, we'd love to meet you! **About the Role:** As a Sales Assistant, you will play a key role in supporting the store's daily operations while delivering exceptional service to our customers. You'll be the face of O'Brien's, helping customers find the perfect wine and ensuring that our store runs smoothly. **Your Responsibilities:** * Complete tasks assigned by management to the highest standards, following company policies. * Provide outstanding customer service by greeting and assisting customers with a positive and helpful attitude. * Accurately operate the till system and handle transactions. * Keep stock levels up to date and well\-organized. * Maintain high merchandising standards, ensuring the store is always presentable. * Stay up\-to\-date with product knowledge to better assist customers. **Who We're Looking For:** * Friendly, enthusiastic, and approachable personality. * A genuine passion for wine and a keen interest in learning more. * Strong sales focus with a commitment to exceeding customer expectations. * Hardworking, with a positive attitude and a team\-oriented approach. * Willingness to roll up your sleeves and help with deliveries, displays, and maintaining the store's appearance. * Must be able to work Monday to Fridays **What We Offer You:** **Benefits:** Enjoy Employee discounts on wine and fizz for all our colleagues. Take advantage of our **Bike to Work** scheme. Access **HSF Health Plans** , covering healthcare expenses including dental, optical, and more. Participate in our **Company Pension Scheme** for future financial security. Benefit from our **Refer a Friend** scheme. Explore a variety of **Development Opportunities** through both online and classroom\-based learning, delivered by in\-house and external industry experts. **Training \& Growth:** Take part in **WSET Wine and Spirit Courses** to expand your wine knowledge and earn recognized qualifications in the industry. We offer excellent **career progression** opportunities, with many of our promotions being filled internally. **Our Commitment to Inclusion:** We believe every colleague should feel empowered to be their authentic self at work respected, valued, and included, no matter who they are, where they're based, or what role they play. Inclusion is at the heart of how we operate. When we embrace individuality and diverse perspectives, we foster a sense of belonging\-and quite simply, diverse teams make better decisions and drive greater impact. As a company, we are continuously expanding, but it's the dedication and talent of our people that drives our success. At O'Brien's, we believe in fostering talent and providing ongoing learning opportunities to help you advance in your career. If you're motivated, ready to learn, and excited about working in a vibrant and busy environment, there's plenty of room to grow with us.

title: Planner
description: **Job Summary** Responsible to serve as the customer interface for the demand plan and shipment information, develop feasible operational plan and maintain ownership over the execution of the operational plan, achieve the objective of customer satisfaction, minimize Jabil liability and continuously improve the performance of the planning metrics. **Essential Duties And Responsibilities** * Work with BU and Customer to Develop a collaborative demand plan through properly executing the Demand Management Process * Create a Master Schedule through resource analysis including material sizing and capacity sizing * Load Master Schedule into the Jabil ERP system. * Create and maintain a feasible production plan and closely work with operation team to achieve successful execution of the plan. * Develop revenue forecast and closely monitor the actual performance and drive for immediate corrective action and recovery plan in case there is potential miss to the revenue target. * Monitor planning metrics and drive for continuous improvement * Comply and follow all procedures within the company security policy and the rules of the road * May perform other duties and responsibilities as assigned **Management \& Supervisory Responsibilities** * Typically reports to Management . Direct supervisor job title(s) typically include: Planning Supervisor, Planning Manager. * Job is NOT directly responsible for managing other employees (e.g., hiring/termination and/or pay decisions, performance management). Please do not change any wording in this section. Only include who the direct supervisor is. **Job Qualifications** KNOWLEDGE REQUIREMENTS * Thorough knowledge of ERP/MRP * 1 to 2 years Materials related experiences * Advanced PC skills including knowledge of Jabil's software packages * Ability to read and interpret documents such as safety rules, operating and maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals. * Ability to write routine reports and correspondence. * Ability to speak effectively before groups of customers or employees of organization, strong communication skills * Ability to apply common sense understanding to carry out instructions furnished in written, oral, or diagram form. * Ability to deal with problems involving several concrete variables in standardized situations. * Ability to calculate figures and amounts such as discounts, interest, commissions, proportions, percentages, area, circumference, and volume. * Ability to apply concepts of basic algebra and geometry. * Strong proficiency in determining logistics requirements to enable company’s business goals and objectives with ability to devise and implement strategy to achieve targets. * Proficient Verbal and Written English Skill **Education \& Experience Requirements** Bachelor’s degree \+ 2 years of experience, or Masters without experience. Or an equivalent combination of education, training, or experience. , BE AWARE OF FRAUD: When applying for a job at Jabil you will be contacted via correspondence through our official job portal with a jabil.com e\-mail address; direct phone call from a member of the Jabil team; or direct e\-mail with a jabil.com e\-mail address. Jabil does not request payments for interviews or at any other point during the hiring process. Jabil will not ask for your personal identifying information such as a social security number, birth certificate, financial institution, driver’s license number or passport information over the phone or via e\-mail. If you believe you are a victim of identity theft, contact your local police department. Any scam job listings should be reported to whatever website it was posted in.

title: Policy Specialist
description: Spencer Recruitment is partnering with our client, a specialist public affairs and research consultancy, to appoint a Policy Specialist/Account Manager. This is a focused, purposeful role for a driven professional ready to transition from broad project management into deep subject\-matter expertise. The successful candidate will immerse themselves in the technology and healthcare sectors, mastering the policies, frameworks, innovations, and parliamentary movements that shape them at an Irish and EU level. **About the Job** * Specialised research role embedded within a dynamic public affairs consultancy * Hybrid working model * Opportunity to develop into a recognised Subject Matter Expert in tech and health policy at an Irish and EU level **The Role** * Drive rigorous, proactive policy intelligence across the technology and healthcare sectors * Utilise, train, and refine advanced AI monitoring tools to track parliamentary debates, committee activity, and legislative drafts * Transform complex policy documents and datasets into sharp, actionable intelligence and strategic narratives * Take full ownership of research output \- from initial data extraction through to final, polished insights **Key Responsibilities** * Conduct ongoing research into regulatory shifts, legislative changes, and emerging public affairs trends in tech and health * Monitor and analyse parliamentary debates, committee work, and legislative developments at Irish and EU level * Produce clear, high\-quality written reports and briefings for internal and client use * Manage the end\-to\-end research process with accuracy and strong attention to detail * Maintain and refine AI\-powered monitoring workflows to improve intelligence gathering * Contribute to the development of strategic narratives and client\-ready policy outputs * Stay ahead of sector developments and proactively flag relevant emerging issues **About You** * 2\-4 years' experience in policy, public affairs, research, PR, or a related consultancy or agency environment * Genuine interest in how public policy, parliamentary action, and technological innovation intersect * Highly organised with a proven ability to manage projects and deliver to deadlines * Comfortable working with new software and AI\-driven monitoring tools * Entrepreneurial mindset \- proactive, comfortable with ambiguity, and driven to find answers * Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to present complex legislative findings clearly and confidently * Analytically minded with a natural aptitude for research and synthesis * Thrives in a startup or fast\-moving environment **What's on Offer** * Highly competitive base salary relative to experience, designed to attract top\-tier talent * Annual performance bonus tied to individual impact and company success * Clear Subject Matter Expert career trajectory in tech and health public affairs * Travel opportunities to attend key industry conferences, policy briefings, and stakeholder events * Supportive, entrepreneurial team environment with real ownership and autonomy

title: Portfolio Associate - Leased, DCC Communities
description: **Description** AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. The AWS Global Data Center Supply Solutions Team is looking for an experienced Portfolio Associate based in the either London or Dublin. In this role, you will be a key member of the Strategic Portfolio Management (SPM) team responsible for tracking and analyzing the operational, financial and contractual performance of our expanding portfolio in Europe, Africa and the Middle East. Portfolio Associates work closely with lessors/vendors and internal partner teams to ensure accuracy of our portfolio data points in order to mitigate risks and support portfolio optimization. **Our Portfolio Managers** * Are detail oriented * Work independently in an extremely fast\-paced environment with a high degree of ambiguity. Key job responsibilities * Review, audit and process rent in line with internal Spend \& Transaction Policies and contractual terms to ensure landlord/vendor adherence * Responsible for creating and managing weekly, monthly or quarterly vendor and partner reviews * Maintain positive relationships with internal stakeholders and deliver results requiring collaboration and coordination with multiple teams * Onboarding and tracking of payment setup for all new landlords/vendors * Interpret complex commercial contract language related to contractual obligations, SLAs, rent, tax, insurance and maintenance clauses * Review of contract related documentation and abstraction of critical information. * Assist leased team with various other administrative tasks **About The Team** Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, , and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in Business, Management, Real Estate, or another related field * Experience utilizing commercial real estate software platforms such as Tango, Yardi, or MRI **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience reading and interpreting lease language, managing critical lease dates, and conducting lease negotiations * Experience with lease administration processes * Experience in commercial real estate or lease administration within an industrial, commercial, and/or data center environment Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. title: Pre-Sales Solutions Engineer (Italian Speaking)
description: *At Klaviyo, we value the unique backgrounds, experiences and perspectives each Klaviyo (we call ourselves Klaviyos) brings to our workplace each and every day. We believe everyone deserves a fair shot at success and appreciate the experiences each person brings beyond the traditional job requirements. If you’re a close but not exact match with the description, we hope you’ll still consider applying. Want to learn more about life at Klaviyo? Visit* *klaviyo.com/careers* *to see how we empower creators to own their own destiny.* **Pre\-Sales Solutions Engineer** ================================= *Commercial \| EMEA \| Italian Speaking* **About the Opportunity** ========================= Solutions Engineers at Klaviyo are technical sellers at the heart of our revenue motion. We don't just demo software. We work as trusted advisors, helping fast\-growing commercial businesses understand how Klaviyo can solve their real challenges, and then we prove it. We're looking for a Pre\-Sales Solutions Engineer to join our EMEA Commercial team, covering the Entrepreneur and SMB segment. This is a great opportunity for someone early in their pre\-sales career who is curious, eager to learn, and excited by the intersection of technology and sales. You don't need to have all the answers on day one. What matters is that you ask great questions, pick things up quickly, and genuinely want to help customers succeed. You'll work alongside Account Executives across the Italian\-speaking market and wider EMEA region, supporting the technical side of deals and building your skills in a collaborative, high\-energy team environment. **What We Do as Solutions Engineers** ===================================== We're technical sellers who drive revenue by winning Commercial deals. Our job is to own the technical win. That means building champions, demonstrating clear value, and making sure prospects choose Klaviyo. We work directly with customers to design solutions that integrate into their existing tech stacks, taking a consultative approach that builds confidence and gets buy\-in from both technical and business stakeholders. Our team builds beyond the day\-to\-day too. Recent projects include AI\-powered demo data generators, a Chrome extension for troubleshooting API calls, and templatised mobile apps to showcase our SDK capabilities. We're a team that builds things to solve problems, and we'd love you to grow into that. **How You'll Make a Difference** ================================ **Support the Technical Win** ----------------------------- * Work alongside Account Executives to support the technical side of Commercial opportunities from qualification through to close. * Participate in technical discovery conversations, learning how to uncover a prospect's goals, current tools, and key constraints. * Deliver product demos and presentations to business and technical audiences, connecting Klaviyo's capabilities to what the customer actually cares about. * Help scope proofs of concept and pilot projects, supporting delivery and keeping stakeholders informed along the way. * Learn how to handle common objections and navigate competitive questions with guidance from the broader team. **Build Customer Confidence** ----------------------------- * Develop a strong understanding of Klaviyo's platform, APIs, and integrations so you can answer technical questions clearly and confidently. * Build relationships with prospects and support them on topics like data strategy, integrations, and implementation approach. * Collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Support on RFPs, security reviews, and technical evaluations where needed. * Start building knowledge of the competitive landscape so you can add value in deal conversations over time. **Contribute to the Team** -------------------------- * Help build and improve repeatable assets like demo narratives, templates, and playbooks that make the wider team more effective. * Use AI tools to support your work, from research and demo prep to light automation. * Share what you're hearing from customers back to Product and GTM teams, especially around gaps and trends in the SMB segment. **Who You Are** =============== **Required** ------------ * Fluent in Italian and English, written and spoken. * 1\+ years of experience in a pre\-sales, solutions engineering, technical sales, or customer\-facing technical role at a B2B SaaS company. * Curiosity about technology and a genuine interest in how businesses use data and marketing tools to grow. * Comfortable talking to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders and adapting your communication style accordingly. * Good at explaining things clearly, whether that's a technical concept to a marketer or a business problem to a developer. * Willing to run live product demos and get comfortable thinking on your feet when questions come up. * A proactive mindset and willingness to learn quickly in a fast\-moving environment. **Nice to Have** ---------------- * Some familiarity with web technologies, REST APIs, or data integrations, even if you haven't worked with them professionally. * Exposure to marketing technology, eCommerce platforms (e.g. Shopify, Magento), or customer engagement tools. * Basic experience with JavaScript, Python, or SQL for exploring data or light integration work. * Familiarity with AI\-assisted tools (e.g. Claude, Cursor) and an interest in using them to work smarter. * Any experience in a commercial or SMB\-focused environment, whether in sales, support, or a technical capacity. * Additional spoken European languages **What Sets You Apart** ----------------------- * You ask great questions and genuinely listen to the answers. * You're not afraid to say you don't know something, but you always follow up with the answer. * You pick up new tools and concepts quickly and enjoy figuring things out. * You take ownership of your work and follow through without needing to be chased. * You're excited about the idea of building a career in pre\-sales and see this role as a serious starting point. **How We'll Measure Success** ============================= * Contribution to technical win rate in the Commercial segment. * Quality of demos, discovery conversations, and handoffs to Customer Success and implementation teams. * Speed and consistency in picking up product knowledge and ramping into deals. * Contributions to team assets and the feedback you bring back from customer conversations. \#LI\-Hybrid We use Covey as part of our hiring and / or promotional process. For jobs or candidates in NYC, certain features may qualify it as an AEDT. As part of the evaluation process we provide Covey with job requirements and candidate submitted applications. We began using Covey Scout for Inbound on April 3, 2025\. Please see the independent bias audit report covering our use of Covey here Our salary range reflects the cost of labour in the country where the job post is advertised. The base salary offered for this position is determined by several factors, including the applicant’s job\-related skills, relevant experience, education or training, and work location. In addition to base salary, our total compensation package may include participation in the company’s annual cash bonus plan, variable compensation (OTE) for sales and customer success roles, equity, sign\-on payments, and a comprehensive range of health, welfare, and wellbeing benefits based on eligibility. Your recruiter can provide more details about the specific salary/OTE range for your preferred location during the hiring process. Base Pay Range in Local Currency: €54\.400—€81\.600 EUR*This role may require up to 10% travel for purposes such as new hire onboarding, client or partner work if applicable, team meetings, and industry events. Travel is coordinated in advance.* **Get to Know Klaviyo** We’re Klaviyo (pronounced clay\-vee\-oh). We empower creators to own their destiny by making first\-party data accessible and actionable like never before. We see limitless potential for the technology we’re developing to nurture personalized experiences in ecommerce and beyond. To reach our goals, we need our own crew of remarkable creators—ambitious and collaborative teammates who stay focused on our north star: delighting our customers. If you’re ready to do the best work of your career, where you’ll be welcomed as your whole self from day one and supported with generous benefits, we hope you’ll join us. *AI fluency at Klaviyo includes responsible use of AI (including privacy, security, bias awareness, and human\-in\-the\-loop). We provide accommodations as needed.* *By participating in Klaviyo’s interview process, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and will adhere to our* *Guidelines for using AI in the Klaviyo interview Process**. For more information about how we process your personal data, see our* *Job Applicant Privacy Notice**.* *Klaviyo is committed to a policy of equal opportunity and non\-discrimination. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, citizenship, national origin, color, religion or religious creed, age, sex (including pregnancy), gender identity, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, veteran or active military status, marital status, criminal record, genetics, retaliation, sexual harassment or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.* *IMPORTANT NOTICE: Our company takes the security and privacy of job applicants very seriously. We will never ask for payment, bank details, or personal financial information as part of the application process. All our legitimate job postings can be found on our official career site. Please be cautious of job offers that come from non\-company email addresses (@klaviyo.com), instant messaging platforms, or unsolicited calls.* **By clicking "Submit Application" you consent to Klaviyo processing your Personal Data in accordance with our Job Applicant Privacy Notice. If you do not wish for Klaviyo to process your Personal Data, please do not submit an application.***You can find our Job Applicant Privacy Notice* *here* *and* *here* *(FR).*

title: Process Specialist
description: **DON’T BE IN THE DARK ABOUT YOUR CAREER…….** **WE WANT YOU TO HELP US CREATE THE DIGITAL FUTURE OF OUR COMPANY** We are looking for exceptional people to join our diverse and multi\-cultural team. This is a fantastic opportunity to play a lead role as we connect process, data and technology, creating sustainable business benefits and drive the achievement of Divisional, Sales Area and Common Function business outcomes. As we are creating a new operating model, this presents a great opportunity for learning and career development in this full\-time position. **We are now recruiting for a Process Specialist (Hybrid) \- for a permanent role in our Dublin Office.** **JOB PURPOSE** The purpose of the Process Specialist role is to represent the business interests and requirements in the Process area by bringing knowledge of current business operations and taking responsibility for helping define how the business will operate in the future, enabled by the new technologies implemented by the programme. This role manages changes that are required in the ‘Plan the Business’ functional area through solution of design, documentation of change, testing and coordinating of change through landscape and through to the deployment and end user training. In this role you will lead and support deployments and help to design, build, test and deploy the Plan the Business solution for Sandvik Mining BA, by working closely with cross stream team members, business members and applicable consulting partners and connected systems. The role requires a deep expertise in SAP and other connected planning connected systems. The role must demonstrate the ability to interpret business challenges across multiple programs and recommend the best solution and practices to improve the end\-to\-end transport solution and business processes. **MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES** * Perform the role of subject matter expert in Demand , Inventory and Supply Management within the ‘ Plan the Business ‘ process area and provide design and roadmap input for the process area. * Provide insight into how the business operates today and ensure that the requirements of the divisions / common functions are known and considered in the design process. * Coordinate system assessment discussions and related activities. * Contribute to and provide input to design and roadmaps for the process area. * Lead the implementation of ‘Plan the Business’ planning solutions, which may involve configuring the software, integrating it with other systems, and ensuring it aligns with business processes. * Peer reviewing change requests relating to the ‘Plan the Business’ space prior to moving forward to Business Area Design Authority approval. * Coordination, management \& approval of transport releases through the test landscape. * Prepare functional specifications for RICEFWs (Reports, interfaces, conversions, extensions, forms and workflows) and peer review as appropriate. * Actively own, promote and improve the technical solution and associated processes for your area. * Validate training material developed by the training team. * Support the Change Management team in assessing business change impacts and engaging with the business teams to drive adoption. * Provide input into resource planning. * Analysing proposed changes to business practices and advising senior management of any compliance implications. * Track, analyse and trend the overall performance of the Transportation Management system. Reviewing issues and completing root cause analysis to reduce issues reoccurring. * Other ad\-hoc duties as requested by management. **Experience** * 7\+ years solution experience, in both SAP and other planning solutions. * Strong knowledge of supply chain management processes and best practices. * Expertise in the configuration and implementation of SAP and other planning tools. * Prior work with integrations and exposure and experience with ETL layer and integration. * S4/HANA knowledge. * Logility Voyager or planning Tool exposure (IBP etc ) advantageous. * ABAP development and debugging knowledge is a plus. * Experience of agile delivery methodology for implementation * Strong communication skills with the ability to effectively interact at all levels within the company. **Education, Licenses \& Certificates** * Educated to degree level or equivalent. * Prior process leadership experience is preferred for role development. * Prior experience in agile methodology is advantageous. * Change management leadership experience is advantageous. * Proficient in MS Office Suite. **What we offer:** * 22 days annual leave * 4 Company Days * Defined Contribution Pension * Onsite gym * VHI Health Insurance * Life \& Disability Insurance * Health \& Wellbeing Initiatives – onsite massage therapist, gym membership, wellbeing seminars, bike to work scheme, etc * Flexible start \& Finish times * Hybrid working **Location**: Dublin, Ireland (Dockline, Mayor Street, IFSC, Dublin 1\) **Reporting to**: Business Process Manager \- Inventory Management **Diversity, Inclusion \& Sustainability** Sandvik has a developed belief in conducting our business in a sustainable and responsible manner, both for our employees and the environment; wherever in the world we operate. Our high ethical standards determine our policy of putting Safety at a premium, acknowledging and respecting the communities and giving due consideration to the environment in the areas we operate. These core values, to prioritise the safety and well\-being of our employees, are the foundation of how we work today. We also recognise the importance on inter\-personal relationships and how those based on honesty, respect and trust, enhance our working lives and help each other’s growth and career development. Equality of opportunity is fully supported and endorsed as an employer by Sandvik, while also welcoming the additional strength that diversity brings, by providing a work environment where everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. **Sandvik Mining and Rock Solutions** Mining is a business area within the Sandvik Group and a global leading supplier of equipment and tools, parts, service, digital solutions and sustainability\-driving technologies for the mining and construction industries. Application areas include rock drilling, rock cutting, loading and hauling, tunneling and quarrying. In 2024, sales were approximately 64 billion SEK with about 17,000 employees. **A glimpse of our culture** At Sandvik, we’re tech driven, innovative and entrepreneurial! For us, success is a team effort \- we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive culture where people can be themselves and reach their full potential. So, we invest in supporting each other, learning together and celebrating our differences. We also give you the freedom to find that perfect mix between work, family life and interests. Visit our stories hub, LinkedIn or Facebook to get to know us further. **How to apply**? Interested in the challenge? Please apply via this advert or directly at https://www.home.sandvik/career Prior to this recruitment, we have already decided which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to utilise, and we respectfully decline any contact from marketing or recruitment agencies regarding additional channels or campaigns. **The closing date for applications is 17th June 2026\.** **Applicants must be eligible to work in the EU or hold a valid permit to work in the EU**.

The role must demonstrate the ability to interpret business challenges across multiple programs and recommend the best solution and practices to improve the end\-to\-end transport solution and business processes. **Main Responsibilities** * Perform the role of subject matter expert in Demand , Inventory and Supply Management within the ‘ Plan the Business ‘ process area and provide design and roadmap input for the process area. * Provide insight into how the business operates today and ensure that the requirements of the divisions / common functions are known and considered in the design process. * Coordinate system assessment discussions and related activities. * Contribute to and provide input to design and roadmaps for the process area. * Lead the implementation of ‘Plan the Business’ planning solutions, which may involve configuring the software, integrating it with other systems, and ensuring it aligns with business processes. * Peer reviewing change requests relating to the ‘Plan the Business’ space prior to moving forward to Business Area Design Authority approval. * Coordination, management \& approval of transport releases through the test landscape. * Prepare functional specifications for RICEFWs (Reports, interfaces, conversions, extensions, forms and workflows) and peer review as appropriate. * Actively own, promote and improve the technical solution and associated processes for your area. * Validate training material developed by the training team. * Support the Change Management team in assessing business change impacts and engaging with the business teams to drive adoption. * Provide input into resource planning. * Analysing proposed changes to business practices and advising senior management of any compliance implications. * Track, analyse and trend the overall performance of the Transportation Management system. Reviewing issues and completing root cause analysis to reduce issues reoccurring. * Other ad\-hoc duties as requested by management. **Experience** * 7\+ years solution experience, in both SAP and other planning solutions. * Strong knowledge of supply chain management processes and best practices. * Expertise in the configuration and implementation of SAP and other planning tools. * Prior work with integrations and exposure and experience with ETL layer and integration. * S4/HANA knowledge. * Logility Voyager or planning Tool exposure (IBP etc ) advantageous. * ABAP development and debugging knowledge is a plus. * Experience of agile delivery methodology for implementation * Strong communication skills with the ability to effectively interact at all levels within the company. **Education, Licenses \& Certificates** * Educated to degree level or equivalent. * Prior process leadership experience is preferred for role development. * Prior experience in agile methodology is advantageous. * Change management leadership experience is advantageous. * Proficient in MS Office Suite. **What we offer:** * 22 days annual leave * 4 Company Days * Defined Contribution Pension * Onsite gym * VHI Health Insurance * Life \& Disability Insurance * Health \& Wellbeing Initiatives – onsite massage therapist, gym membership, wellbeing seminars, bike to work scheme, etc * Flexible start \& Finish times * Hybrid working **Location** : Dublin, Ireland (Dockline, Mayor Street, IFSC, Dublin 1\) **Reporting to** : Business Process Manager \- Inventory Management **Diversity, Inclusion \& Sustainability** Sandvik has a developed belief in conducting our business in a sustainable and responsible manner, both for our employees and the environment; wherever in the world we operate. Our high ethical standards determine our policy of putting Safety at a premium, acknowledging and respecting the communities and giving due consideration to the environment in the areas we operate. These core values, to prioritise the safety and well\-being of our employees, are the foundation of how we work today. We also recognise the importance on inter\-personal relationships and how those based on honesty, respect and trust, enhance our working lives and help each other’s growth and career development. Equality of opportunity is fully supported and endorsed as an employer by Sandvik, while also welcoming the additional strength that diversity brings, by providing a work environment where everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. **Sandvik Mining and Rock Solutions** Mining is a business area within the Sandvik Group and a global leading supplier of equipment and tools, parts, service, digital solutions and sustainability\-driving technologies for the mining and construction industries. Application areas include rock drilling, rock cutting, loading and hauling, tunneling and quarrying. In 2024, sales were approximately 64 billion SEK with about 17,000 employees. **A glimpse of our culture** At Sandvik, we’re tech driven, innovative and entrepreneurial! For us, success is a team effort \- we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive culture where people can be themselves and reach their full potential. So, we invest in supporting each other, learning together and celebrating our differences. We also give you the freedom to find that perfect mix between work, family life and interests. Visit our stories hub, LinkedIn or Facebook to get to know us further. **How to apply** ? Interested in the challenge? Please apply via this advert or directly at https://www.home.sandvik/career Prior to this recruitment, we have already decided which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to utilise, and we respectfully decline any contact from marketing or recruitment agencies regarding additional channels or campaigns. **The closing date for applications is 17th June 2026\.** **Applicants must be eligible to work in the EU or hold a valid permit to work in the EU** .

title: Product Development Associate
description: **JOB TITLE:** Product Development Associate: DIRECT TO RETAIL **LOCATION:** Dublin **REPORTS TO:** Manager Prod. Dev. EMEA – Home \& Fashion **SUMMARY OF POSITION :** Provides creative support for key DTR Fashion retailers through design briefs and point of view boards to increase 365 Fashion Business. Reviews designs and products submitted by key DTR Fashion retailer through WB product development/approval process across Fashion \& Home. Ensures submissions and product designs featuring any Warner Bros. owned properties are reviewed within a contractually stipulated timeframe to ensure compliance with Consumer Products policies. Works with Softline’s Hub to support \& produce compelling product which best represents WB brands at retail. Undertake research projects with the PD team to maintain knowledge of the competitive landscape. Support Creative in London with the creation of Mood boards, toolkits, comp shops, Fashion graphics to inspire key licensees as required Visit retailer offices as agreed to give onsite advice and approval. **JOB RESPONSIBILITIES :** * Work closely with DTR Softline’s Hub to achieve best results. * Provide creative support tailored to WBD business needs through DTR briefs and point of view boards to inspire key fashion retailers. * Provide concept CADS, design and product development support for key DTR fashion retailers where required. * Reviews samples and designs to ensure all submissions meet the brand guidelines and legal requirements. * Acts as the brand ambassador on all product submissions making decisions based on whether the product is in line with brand strategy, creative strategy, and product quality standards. * Approves or rejects based on the creative standard and legal guidelines set. * With knowledge of the manufacturing process and a knowledge of Warner Bros. policies and procedures and brand guidelines reviews and provides detailed comments on concept, pre\-production, and final samples submitted by licensees on items including product, packaging, marketing, point of sale. * Maintains a one\-on\-one relationship with EMEA counterparts, as well Designers, PD, Commercial, Franchise Teams etc. to ensure understanding and a smooth working approval process. * Drives creative, trend\-driven product initiatives and newness that contribute directly to agreed KPIs, revenue targets and wider business goals, in close partnership with commercial counterparts. **JOB QUALIFICATION STANDARDS** **Work Experience** * Minimum 3 years\-experience in product development, design. * Fashion and Home experience a must. * Design Degree or qualification preferred. * Experience working with Highstreet fashion retailers preferred. **Knowledge/Skills** * PC Skills: Windows, Microsoft Word and Excel required. Working Knowledge of Adobe Photoshop. Illustrator and In\-Design * Must have the ability to absorb and respond quickly to given information. * Must have the ability to learn the various guidelines relating to WB Intellectual properties and distinguish different styles of art from others to accurately log and review submissions. * Must have the ability to learn about art production and manufacturing processes in general. * Must be detailed and have an artistic eye. * Must have the ability to learn about handling digital and ai images. * Must have good spelling, grammar, punctuation, and English usage and be able to accurately describe images in words and accurately record all verbiage in the database correctly. * Must be able to sift through information and identify priorities. * Must be able to interface professionally with key fashion retailers, buyers, departments, licensees, and all levels of management. Other Requirements * Excellent organizational abilities. * Must have the ability to acquire and work with knowledge of copyrights and trademarks for use in product approvals. * Must be able to work independently. * Must be able to communicate effectively and tactfully with all levels of personnel, inside and outside of the company. * Must be able to work in a fast\-paced environment and prioritize work effectively.

title: Project Analyst
description: **Job Title** Project Analyst **Job Description Summary** The Project Analyst is a support role for our Real Estate Accounting Operations and Compliance team. The successful candidate will play a crucial role in reconciling financial data, managing purchase orders, and assisting in the overall budget management for various projects/programs within the organization. This position involves close collaboration with program and project owners as well as finance and procurement. **Job Description** **POSITION REQUIRED RESPONSIBILITIES** **Reconciliation Of Project/Program Spend** l Conduct regular reconciliations of project and program spend to ensure accuracy and compliance with established policies \& procedures. l Identify and rectify discrepancies in financial records, providing timely resolution. **Purchase Order Management** l Collaborate with workplace services partners, project managers, and procurement teams to create and manage purchase orders and change orders. l Track and verify purchase orders against budget allocations, ensuring alignment with outlined spend categories. **Budget Management** l Assist in the development and monitoring of project and program budgets, working closely with workplace services partners, project managers, finance team and program owners. l Provide regular updates on budget status, highlighting any variances, and proposing corrective actions as needed. **Financial Reporting** l Prepare and generate financial reports related to project and program expenditures and budget performance. l Contribute to the analysis of financial data to support decision\-making processes. **Documentation And Compliance** l Maintain accurate and organized financial documentation for audit and compliance purposes. l Ensure adherence to accounting standards, policies, and procedures. l Validate quarterly fixed asset reports. l Assist in the development/enhancement of Standard Operating Procedures (SOP’s) around project and program reporting and reconciliations l Secure appropriate approvals for invoices in accordance with clients policy **Collaboration** l Work closely with cross\-functional teams, including project managers, workplace services partners, procurement, finance colleagues, and program owners, to gather relevant financial information and facilitate seamless communication. **Skills/Qualities Required For Position** l Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. l Ability and efficiency to focus on achieving thoroughness and accuracy when accomplishing tasks, no matter how small or large. l Excellent organizational abilities l Proficient in accounting\-related software (Oracle, Coupa preferred). l High proficiency in Microsoft Excel/Word, Google Suites and other software applications l Effective communication and interpersonal skills. l Proactive and self\-motivated. l Ability to work both independently and as part of a team. l Strong commitment to maintaining confidentiality. l Eagerness to learn and grow within the role. **Minimum Education \& Experience** l Knowledge of basic accounting principles. l Familiarity with project management concepts. l Previous experience with financial reconciliation and budget tracking is a plus. l Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. l 3\-5 years experience in a Finance and/or Accounting\-related role INCO: “Cushman \& Wakefield”

title: Project Coordinator GPOS Controls (12-Months FTC)
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Do as** **Project Co\-ordinator GPOS Controls** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Support the successful delivery of Global Point of Sale (GPOS) Controls by coordinating complex project activities, driving planning excellence, and ensuring strong governance and delivery discipline. * Prepare governance reporting packs, track project status, risks, issues, and dependencies, capture meeting minutes, and monitor completion of actions. * Develop and maintain integrated project and programme plans, milestones, dependencies, and deliverables, ensuring accurate updates in Planisware and POAP formats. * Proactively identify, track, and escalate risks, issues, assumptions, and dependencies through a centrally managed RAID log aligned across workstreams. * Operate structured change control processes, maintaining change request logs and managing impacts to scope, timeline, and approvals. * Act as a trusted advisor to workstream leads, build strong stakeholder relationships, and drive continuous improvement through automation (e.g., Copilot) and lessons learned. **What You’ll Get** This role is a hybrid opportunity, offering 3 days Working from home. People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Pension, 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * 2–5 years of experience supporting technology‑enabled transformation programmes, with a strong grasp of software delivery lifecycles, project management methodologies, and emerging technologies. * Hands‑on experience using project management and delivery tools such as Planisware, Jira, or Microsoft Project, alongside data analysis and reporting tools including Excel, Power BI, or Tableau. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate complex technical and financial information to both technical teams and senior stakeholders. * Strong analytical and problem‑solving capabilities, enabling effective identification of issues and development of practical solutions in fast‑paced environments. * Proven planning, organisation, and multitasking skills, with the ability to prioritise workloads and deliver to agreed timescales. * Collaborative team player with the confidence to challenge constructively, complemented by desirable experience in Retail domains (e.g. Supply Chain, Digital, Store Operations) and project management certifications such as PMP, PRINCE2, APM, Scrum Master, or SAFe. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-7817

title: Psychologist – Childrens Disability Network Team
description: **Psychologist \- Childrens Disability Network Team** **Permanent Contract \- 35 hours per week** **Location: Athy/Kildare Town, Co. Kildare** **Clinical Psychologist Salary Scale €63,222 to €108,234** **DOE** The South Kildare West Wicklow CDNT operate a biopsychosocial model and adopted a Family Centred Approach in line with the National Framework for Progressing Disability Services. The CDNT provide supports across South Kildare and West Wicklow. The psychologist will work as part of an inter\-disciplinary team to provide clinical supports to children and young people aged 0\-18 years who present with complex needs associated with a disability. **Responsibilities** * Selection, administration and analysis of a range of standardise and non\-standardised assessments of cognitive ability, emotional well\-being and behaviour. * Case formulation that informs programme/intervention development to include individual learning programmes and skills training * Development of behaviour management plans and provision of support regarding the implementation of same * Facilitation of group interventions and individual interventions for service users, their families or staff * Participate in continuing professional development including in\-service training, attending or presenting at conferences/courses relevant to practice **Requirements** * Hold a university degree or diploma (QQ1 level 8 equivalent) obtained with 1st or 2nd class honours in which Psychology was taken as a major subject and honours obtained in that subject AND have an Irish post\-graduate professional psychology qualification accredited by the Psychological Society of Ireland in Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychology or an equivalent qualification in Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychology from another jurisdiction validated by the Department of Health. * Demonstrate competence in working with intellectual disabilities \- either at least 60 days or equivalent supervised clinical placements as part of the professional qualification; or at least 60 days or equivalent post\-qualification supervised work experience as a psychologist. * Experience of working with children with a disability * Good communication and interpersonal skills * Must have a full, valid driver's licence and access to your own vehicle * Must be eligible to work in Ireland without sponsorship **Benefits** * 29 days annual leave * Paid Maternity/Paternity Leave (subject to service) * Federation of Voluntary Bodies Pension Scheme: Employer 7% \& Employee 5% contributions * Optional Salary Protection Plan * Rewarding work with flexible hours * Employee Assistance Programme * ESDA.ie and local Employee Discounts * Personal Development, Professional Growth and Career Progression Closing date for applications is 17th June 2026\. A detailed Job Description is available on request. Informal enquires to Catherine Byrne. Shortlisting will be completed on the basis of information provided in your application against the requirement of the role. The successful candidate will be required to provide satisfactory references and Garda Vetting and overseas police vetting, where applicable. Salary is paid in accordance with the Department of Health Consolidated Salary Scales. **About Kare** Kare delivers a wide range of community\-based supports to children and adults with intellectual disabilities in an innovative and progressive way. Our goal is to support them and their families to create a present and future that fulfils their personal potential. **Our Values: Respect, Rights, Collaboration, Quality, Person Centred.** Visit our website: Home \| Kare *Kare is an Equal Opportunities Employer, committed to a policy of equal opportunity and encourage applications under all nine grounds of the Employment Equality Act. We are committed to providing accommodations for applicants with disabilities. If you require any adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know*
